title: Iraqi judiciary reports role in thwarting вҖҳterrorist plotвҖҷ in Hamburg
title_en: Iraqi judiciary reports role in thwarting вҖҳterrorist plotвҖҷ in Hamburg
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - IraqвҖҷs Supreme Judicial Council (SJC) on Tuesday stated that it provided German authorities with вҖңimportant information and evidenceвҖқ that helped thwart a premeditated вҖңterrorist plotвҖқ in the city of Hamburg in northern Germany. The Council, in a statement, cited the National Center for International Judicial Cooperation as confirming that вҖңjudicial coordination and information-sharing with German counterparts played a key role in foiling a dangerous terrorist plot in the city of Hamburg, which was at an advanced stage of preparation and aimed at targeting civilian gatherings in the city.вҖқ The Iraqi judiciary provided the information, which вҖңcontributed to supporting monitoring and investigation proceduresвҖқ that enabled German law enforcement agencies to take вҖңurgent measures to prevent the attack before it was executed,вҖқ the statement added. Of note, the National Center for International Judicial Cooperation is a specialized department within the SJC that serves as the primary diplomatic and legal bridge between IraqвҖҷs domestic court system and the global legal community. The SJC statement quoted the head of the Center, Ali Hussein Jaffat, as saying the coordination was conducted вҖңin line with the directives of Iraqi Chief Justice Faiq Zidan, who ordered the strengthening of international judicial cooperation channels and the exchange of information with friendly countries, particularly in cases related to combating terrorism and transnational organized crime.вҖқ вҖңThe success of these efforts reflects the importance of joint judicial and security coordination in foiling terrorist plots before they occur, protecting civilian lives, and enhancing security and stability at both the national and international levels,вҖқ Jaffat added. Interestingly, the development coincided with the announcement by German prosecutors that a 17-year-old Syrian national was arrested in Hamburg вҖңon suspicion of planning an attack likely inspired by the Islamic State

title: ЩӮЪ•Ъ©ШұШҜЩҶЫҢ Ш¬ШұШ¬ ЩҲ Щ…ШҙЪ© ЩҲЫ•Ъ© ШұЫҺЪ©Ш§ШұЫҺЪ© ШЁЫҶ ШұЫҺЪҜШұЫҢЪ©ШұШҜЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШЁЪөШ§ЩҲШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЪӨШ§ЫҢШұЫҶШіЫҢ ЩҮШ§ЩҶШӘШ§ ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЩҫЫҺШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШӘ
title_en: ЩӮЪ•Ъ©ШұШҜЩҶЫҢ Ш¬ШұШ¬ ЩҲ Щ…ШҙЪ© ЩҲЫ•Ъ© ШұЫҺЪ©Ш§ШұЫҺЪ© ШЁЫҶ ШұЫҺЪҜШұЫҢЪ©ШұШҜЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШЁЪөШ§ЩҲШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЪӨШ§ЫҢШұЫҶШіЫҢ ЩҮШ§ЩҶШӘШ§ ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЩҫЫҺШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШӘ
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ ШЁЫ•ЩҫЫҺЫҢ ЩҶЩҲЫҺШӘШұЫҢЩҶ ШІШ§ЩҶЫҢШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶШҢ ЩҫШҙЪ©ЩҶЫҢЩҶЫҢ ЩҫШІЫҢШҙЪ©ЫҢЫҢ ЪҜЫ•ШҙШӘЫҢШ§ШұЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШҰЫ•Щ…ШұЫҢЪ©ЫҢ ЩҲ Ш®Ш§ЩҶЩ…ЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ЩҒЫ•Ъ•Ы•ЩҶШіЫҢ Ъ©Ы• Щ„Ы•ЩҲ Ъ©Ы•ШҙШӘЫҢЫҢЫ• ЪҜЫ•ШҙШӘЫҢШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•ШҜШ§ ШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶ Ъ©Ы• ЪӨШ§ЫҢШұЫҶШіЫҢ "ЩҮШ§ЩҶШӘШ§"ЫҢ ШӘЫҺШҜШ§ ШЁЪөШ§ЩҲШЁЩҲЩҲЫ•ШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ•ШҢ ЪҜЫ•Ъ•Ш§ЩҲЩҶЫ•ШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҫЫҶШІЫ•ШӘЫҢЪӨ ШҜЫ•ШұЪҶЩҲЩҲЩҶ ЩҲ ШЁЫ• ЩҒЫ•ШұЩ…ЫҢ ШӘЩҲЩҲШҙШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШұШ§ЪҜЫ•ЫҢЫ•ЩҶШҜШұШ§ШҢ ЩҮЫ•ШұЩҲЫ•ЩҮШ§ ЪҜЫ•ШҙШӘЫҢШ§ШұЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШӘШұЫҢ ШҰЫ•Щ…ШұЫҢЪ©ЫҢШҙ ЩҶЫҢШҙШ§ЩҶЫ•ЫҢ ШіЩҲЩҲЪ©ЫҢ ЩҶЫ•Ш®ЫҶШҙЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ы•ЫҢ ШӘЫҺШҜШ§ ШҜЫ•ШұЪ©Ы•ЩҲШӘЩҲЩҲЫ•.

title: US offers $15 million reward for info on IRGC oil shipments, facilitators
title_en: US offers $15 million reward for info on IRGC oil shipments, facilitators
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - The US Department of State on Tuesday announced a reward of up to $15 million for information on oil shipments linked to IranвҖҷs Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and its facilitators. The move comes a day after Washington said it was вҖңintensifying pressureвҖқ on the IRGC by sanctioning its oil shipment and sales arm. The DepartmentвҖҷs Rewards for Justice Program issued a statement titled вҖңReward of up to $15 million for information on IRGC oil sales,вҖқ noting that the IRGC вҖңgenerates revenue for its activities through various illicit operations, including the sale and shipment of sanctioned Iranian oil.вҖқ It added, вҖңIf you have information about the IRGCвҖҷs oil shipments, the tankers or entities involved in such illicit oil transfers, or related activities or individuals, contact us. Your information could make you eligible for a reward and relocation.вҖқ A day earlier, State Department Deputy Spokesperson Tommy Pigott said in a Monday statement that the administration of President Donald Trump is вҖңintensifying pressureвҖқ on the IRGC by вҖңtargeting the financial networks that enable its illicit oil operations.вҖқ He said the US is sanctioning вҖңa network involved in selling and shipping Iranian oil to overseas buyers on behalf of the IRGC.вҖқ as well as вҖңthree senior officials from the IRGCвҖҷs Shahid PurjaвҖҷfari Oil Headquarters who coordinate these transactions.вҖқ The Shahid PurjaвҖҷfari Oil Headquarters is an IRGC-affiliated unit responsible for managing and coordinating the shipment and sale of Iranian oil. Pigott added that the sanctions aim to disrupt IRGC oil operations, which he said вҖңfinance IranвҖҷs support for terrorist proxies and regional aggression,вҖқ describing them as an вҖңadditional round of sanctions under Economic FuryвҖқ against Tehran. The US launched Operation Epic Fury on February 28, a joint aerial campaign with Israel against Iran, striking more than 17,000 sites over six weeks before a Pakistan-mediated ceasefire was agreed on April 8

title: Iraq denies claims of Israeli вҖҳclandestineвҖҷ base in southern desert
title_en: Iraq denies claims of Israeli вҖҳclandestineвҖҷ base in southern desert
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - The Iraqi defense ministry on Tuesday denied to Rudaw the claims that Israel had set up a вҖңclandestine military outpostвҖқ in a southern Iraq desert, describing the allegation as an exaggerated account of a single engagement that took place in early March. Major General Tahseen al-Khafaji, Director of Media and Moral Guidance at the ministry, said that вҖңon March 3, we were alerted to the presence of armed individualsвҖқ in the desert area between Karbala and Najaf provinces. He added that вҖңwhen Karbala Operations Command units deployed to the site on March 4, they came under aerial bombardment, which killed one Iraqi soldier and wounded two others.вҖқ Khafaji further noted that, while a small foreign force may have briefly established a presence in the area amid the broader regional conflict involving the United States, Israel, and Iran, a follow-up Iraqi mission on March 5 found no evidence of any installation. вҖңThere was no base, no airstrip, nor any sign that any administrative or military work had been conducted there,вҖқ Khafaji affirmed, adding that the origin of the aircraft involved in the March 4 strike remains unconfirmed. The remarks come against the backdrop of a Saturday report by The Wall Street Journal, which stated that вҖңIsrael set up a clandestine military outpost in the Iraqi desert to support its air campaign against Iran and launched airstrikes against Iraqi troops who almost discovered it early in the war, people familiar with the matter, including U.S. officials, said.вҖқ According to the newspaper, the installation вҖңhoused special forces and served as a logistical hub for the Israeli air force, just before the war started with the knowledge of the U.S.вҖқ In a related development on Monday, IraqвҖҷs Joint Operations Command (JOC) issued a firm denial regarding "unauthorized bases or forces" within the country. title: ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЫ•ШЁЫ•ШұЫҢ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…Ы•ЪөШ§ЫҢЫ•ШӘЫҢЫҢ ЩҮЫ•ЩҲЩ„ЫҺШұ: 27 ЩҮЫ•ШІШ§Шұ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЩҫШ§ЩҶЫҢШ§ ЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ШұЪҜЫ• ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЫ•ШЁЫ•ШұЫҢ Ъ©ШұШ§ЩҲЩҶ
title_en: ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЫ•ШЁЫ•ШұЫҢ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…Ы•ЪөШ§ЫҢЫ•ШӘЫҢЫҢ ЩҮЫ•ЩҲЩ„ЫҺШұ: 27 ЩҮЫ•ШІШ§Шұ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЩҫШ§ЩҶЫҢШ§ ЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ШұЪҜЫ• ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЫ•ШЁЫ•ШұЫҢ Ъ©ШұШ§ЩҲЩҶ
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ Щ…Ы•ШіШұЩҲШұ ШЁШ§ШұШІШ§ЩҶЫҢШҢ ШіЫ•ШұЫҶЪ©ЫҢ ШӯЪ©ЩҲЩ…Ы•ШӘЫҢ ЩҮЫ•ШұЫҺЩ…ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҲШұШҜШіШӘШ§ЩҶ ЩҫЫҺШҙЩҲШ§ШІЫҢ Щ„Ы• ШЁШ§ЪөЫҢЫҶШІЫҢ ЩҶЩҲЫҺЫҢ ЩҫШ§ЩҫШ§ЫҢ ЪӨШ§ШӘЫҢЪ©Ш§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮ Ъ©ШұШҜШӣ ШЁШ§ЪөЫҢЫҶШІЫ•Ъ©Ы•Шҙ ЩҫЫ•ЫҢШ§Щ…ЫҢ ЩӮЫ•ШҜШ§ШіЫ•ШӘЫҢ ЩҫШ§ЩҫШ§ЫҢ ЪҜЫ•ЫҢШ§ЩҶШҜ ЩҲ ЪҜЩҲШӘЫҢШҢ "Ъ©ЩҲШұШҜШіШӘШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШҜЪөЫҢ ЩҫШ§ЩҫШ§ШҜШ§ЫҢЫ• ЩҲ ЩҶШІШ§ЫҢ Ш®ЫҺШұ ЩҲ ШҰШ§ШҙШӘЫҢ ШЁЫҶ ШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШӘ."

title: ШұШ§ЩҲЫҺЪҳЪ©Ш§ШұЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШіЩҲЩҲШҜШ§ЩҶЫҢ: ЩҲЫ•ШІШ§ШұЫ•ШӘЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҶЫ•ЩҲШӘШҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұЫ•ШЁШ§ ЩҲ Ъ©ШҙШӘЩҲЪ©Ш§Ъө ШЁЫҶ ШіЩҲЩҲШҜШ§ЩҶЫҢ ЫҢЫ•Ъ©Щ„Ш§ШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶЫ•ШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ•
title_en: ШұШ§ЩҲЫҺЪҳЪ©Ш§ШұЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШіЩҲЩҲШҜШ§ЩҶЫҢ: ЩҲЫ•ШІШ§ШұЫ•ШӘЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҶЫ•ЩҲШӘШҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұЫ•ШЁШ§ ЩҲ Ъ©ШҙШӘЩҲЪ©Ш§Ъө ШЁЫҶ ШіЩҲЩҲШҜШ§ЩҶЫҢ ЫҢЫ•Ъ©Щ„Ш§ШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶЫ•ШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ•
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ ШіШЁЫ•ЫҢ ЩҫЫҺЩҶШ¬ШҙЫ•Щ…Щ…Ы•ШҢ ЩҫЫ•ШұЩ„Ы•Щ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢ Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮ ШЁЫҶ ШҜЫ•ЩҶЪҜШҜШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫ• Ъ©Ш§ШЁЫҢЩҶЫ•Ъ©Ы•ЫҢ Ш№Ы•Щ„ЫҢ ЩҒШ§Щ„Шӯ ШІЫ•ЫҢШҜЫҢ Ъ©ЫҶШҜЫ•ШЁЫҺШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ•

title: ШҰШ§ШіЫҢШ§ШіЫҺЪө 125 Щ…Щ„ЫҢЫҶЩҶ ШҜЫҢЩҶШ§Шұ ШЁЫ• Ъ©Ы•ШұШӘЫҢ ШӘЫ•ЩҶШҜШұЩҲШіШӘЫҢ ЩҲ ЪҶШ§ЩҲШҜЫҺШұЫҢЫҢ Щ…ЩҶШҜШ§ЪөШ§ЩҶ ШҜЫ•ШЁЫ•Ш®ШҙЫҺШӘ
title_en: ШҰШ§ШіЫҢШ§ШіЫҺЪө 125 Щ…Щ„ЫҢЫҶЩҶ ШҜЫҢЩҶШ§Шұ ШЁЫ• Ъ©Ы•ШұШӘЫҢ ШӘЫ•ЩҶШҜШұЩҲШіШӘЫҢ ЩҲ ЪҶШ§ЩҲШҜЫҺШұЫҢЫҢ Щ…ЩҶШҜШ§ЪөШ§ЩҶ ШҜЫ•ШЁЫ•Ш®ШҙЫҺШӘ
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ Щ…Ы•ШіШұЩҲШұ ШЁШ§ШұШІШ§ЩҶЫҢШҢ ШіЫ•ШұЫҶЪ©ЫҢ ШӯЪ©ЩҲЩ…Ы•ШӘЫҢ ЩҮЫ•ШұЫҺЩ…ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҲШұШҜШіШӘШ§ЩҶ ЩҫЫҺШҙЩҲШ§ШІЫҢ Щ„Ы• ШЁШ§ЪөЫҢЫҶШІЫҢ ЩҶЩҲЫҺЫҢ ЩҫШ§ЩҫШ§ЫҢ ЪӨШ§ШӘЫҢЪ©Ш§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮ Ъ©ШұШҜШӣ ШЁШ§ЪөЫҢЫҶШІЫ•Ъ©Ы•Шҙ ЩҫЫ•ЫҢШ§Щ…ЫҢ ЩӮЫ•ШҜШ§ШіЫ•ШӘЫҢ ЩҫШ§ЩҫШ§ЫҢ ЪҜЫ•ЫҢШ§ЩҶШҜ ЩҲ ЪҜЩҲШӘЫҢШҢ "Ъ©ЩҲШұШҜШіШӘШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШҜЪөЫҢ ЩҫШ§ЩҫШ§ШҜШ§ЫҢЫ• ЩҲ ЩҶШІШ§ЫҢ Ш®ЫҺШұ ЩҲ ШҰШ§ШҙШӘЫҢ ШЁЫҶ ШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШӘ."

title: Ъ©Ш§ШіЫҢШ§Ші ШҜЪҳЫҢ ЪҜЫ•Ъ•Ш§ЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ Щ…ЫҶШұЫҢЩҶЫҢЫҶЫҢЫ• ШЁЫҶ ШіЫ•ШұЪ©ШұШҜШ§ЫҢЫ•ШӘЫҢЪ©ШұШҜЩҶЫҢ ШұЫҢШ§Щ„ Щ…Ы•ШҜШұЫҢШҜ
title_en: Ъ©Ш§ШіЫҢШ§Ші ШҜЪҳЫҢ ЪҜЫ•Ъ•Ш§ЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ Щ…ЫҶШұЫҢЩҶЫҢЫҶЫҢЫ• ШЁЫҶ ШіЫ•ШұЪ©ШұШҜШ§ЫҢЫ•ШӘЫҢЪ©ШұШҜЩҶЫҢ ШұЫҢШ§Щ„ Щ…Ы•ШҜШұЫҢШҜ
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ ШіШӘШ§ЩҒЫҢ ШӘЫ•Ъ©ЩҶЫҢЪ©ЫҢЫҢ ЩҮЫ•ЪөШЁЪҳШ§ШұШҜЫ•ЫҢ ШЁЫ•ШұШ§ШІЫҢЩ„ ЪҜЩҲЩ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШҰШ§Щ…Ш§ШҜЫ•ЫҢЫҢ ЩҶЫ•ЫҢЩ…Ш§Шұ ШҜШ§ШіЫҺЩ„ЪӨШ§ ЩҮЫ•ЫҢЫ• ШЁЫҶ Щ…ЫҶЩҶШҜЫҢШ§Щ„ ЩҲ ЪҶЫ•ЩҶШҜ ЩҮЫҶЪ©Ш§ШұЫҺЪ©ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЩҶЫҢШҙШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҲЩҲЫ•ШҢ ШҜЫ•ЪөЫҺЩҶ ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ЩҮЫҶЪ©Ш§ШұШ§ЩҶЫ• ШұЫҺЪҜШұЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШЁШ§ЩҶЪҜЩҮЫҺШҙШӘЪ©ШұШҜЩҶЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢШІШ§ЩҶЫ• ШЁЫҶ Щ…ЫҶЩҶШҜЫҢШ§Щ„.

title: ЩҮЫ•ЪҳЩ…Ш§ШұЫҢ Щ…ЩҶ ШЁЫҶ ШұЩҲЩҲШҜШ§ЩҲ: Щ…ЩҲЩҲЪҶЫ•Ш®ЫҶШұШ§ЩҶ ШҜЫ•ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶ ШЁЪҶЩҶ Щ…ЩҲЩҲЪҶЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұШЁЪҜШұЩҶ
title_en: ЩҮЫ•ЪҳЩ…Ш§ШұЫҢ Щ…ЩҶ ШЁЫҶ ШұЩҲЩҲШҜШ§ЩҲ: Щ…ЩҲЩҲЪҶЫ•Ш®ЫҶШұШ§ЩҶ ШҜЫ•ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶ ШЁЪҶЩҶ Щ…ЩҲЩҲЪҶЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұШЁЪҜШұЩҶ
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ Щ…Ы•ШіШұЩҲШұ ШЁШ§ШұШІШ§ЩҶЫҢШҢ ШіЫ•ШұЫҶЪ©ЫҢ ШӯЪ©ЩҲЩ…Ы•ШӘЫҢ ЩҮЫ•ШұЫҺЩ…ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҲШұШҜШіШӘШ§ЩҶ ЩҫЫҺШҙЩҲШ§ШІЫҢ Щ„Ы• ШЁШ§ЪөЫҢЫҶШІЫҢ ЩҶЩҲЫҺЫҢ ЩҫШ§ЩҫШ§ЫҢ ЪӨШ§ШӘЫҢЪ©Ш§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮ Ъ©ШұШҜШӣ ШЁШ§ЪөЫҢЫҶШІЫ•Ъ©Ы•Шҙ ЩҫЫ•ЫҢШ§Щ…ЫҢ ЩӮЫ•ШҜШ§ШіЫ•ШӘЫҢ ЩҫШ§ЩҫШ§ЫҢ ЪҜЫ•ЫҢШ§ЩҶШҜ ЩҲ ЪҜЩҲШӘЫҢШҢ "Ъ©ЩҲШұШҜШіШӘШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШҜЪөЫҢ ЩҫШ§ЩҫШ§ШҜШ§ЫҢЫ• ЩҲ ЩҶШІШ§ЫҢ Ш®ЫҺШұ ЩҲ ШҰШ§ШҙШӘЫҢ ШЁЫҶ ШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШӘ."

title: Щ…ШӯЫ•Щ…Щ…Ы•ШҜЫҢ 10 ШіШ§ЪөШ§ЩҶ ЩҫЩҲЩҲЪ©Ш§ЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ШҜЫ•Щ…Ш§Шұ ЩҲ Щ…Ш§ШіЩҲЩ„Ъ©Ы•ЫҢ ЩҮЫ•ЫҢЫ•: ЩҮШ§ЩҲЪ©Ш§ШұЫҢЩ… ШЁЪ©Ы•ЩҶ ШӘШ§ЩҲЫ•Ъ©ЩҲ ЪҶШ§ШұЫ•ШіЫ•Шұ ШЁЪ©ШұЫҺЩ…
title_en: Щ…ШӯЫ•Щ…Щ…Ы•ШҜЫҢ 10 ШіШ§ЪөШ§ЩҶ ЩҫЩҲЩҲЪ©Ш§ЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ШҜЫ•Щ…Ш§Шұ ЩҲ Щ…Ш§ШіЩҲЩ„Ъ©Ы•ЫҢ ЩҮЫ•ЫҢЫ•: ЩҮШ§ЩҲЪ©Ш§ШұЫҢЩ… ШЁЪ©Ы•ЩҶ ШӘШ§ЩҲЫ•Ъ©ЩҲ ЪҶШ§ШұЫ•ШіЫ•Шұ ШЁЪ©ШұЫҺЩ…
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ Щ…Ы•ШіШұЩҲШұ ШЁШ§ШұШІШ§ЩҶЫҢШҢ ШіЫ•ШұЫҶЪ©ЫҢ ШӯЪ©ЩҲЩ…Ы•ШӘЫҢ ЩҮЫ•ШұЫҺЩ…ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҲШұШҜШіШӘШ§ЩҶ ЩҫЫҺШҙЩҲШ§ШІЫҢ Щ„Ы• ШЁШ§ЪөЫҢЫҶШІЫҢ ЩҶЩҲЫҺЫҢ ЩҫШ§ЩҫШ§ЫҢ ЪӨШ§ШӘЫҢЪ©Ш§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮ Ъ©ШұШҜШӣ ШЁШ§ЪөЫҢЫҶШІЫ•Ъ©Ы•Шҙ ЩҫЫ•ЫҢШ§Щ…ЫҢ ЩӮЫ•ШҜШ§ШіЫ•ШӘЫҢ ЩҫШ§ЩҫШ§ЫҢ ЪҜЫ•ЫҢШ§ЩҶШҜ ЩҲ ЪҜЩҲШӘЫҢШҢ "Ъ©ЩҲШұШҜШіШӘШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШҜЪөЫҢ ЩҫШ§ЩҫШ§ШҜШ§ЫҢЫ• ЩҲ ЩҶШІШ§ЫҢ Ш®ЫҺШұ ЩҲ ШҰШ§ШҙШӘЫҢ ШЁЫҶ ШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШӘ."

title: ШіЫҺ ШЁЫҢШұЫҢ ЩҲШІЫ•ЫҢ ЪҜЫ•ШұЩ…ЫҢЫҢ ЪҳЫҺШұШІЫ•ЩҲЫҢ ШЁЫ• ЩӮЩҲЩҲЪөШ§ЫҢЫҢ 900 Щ…Ы•ШӘШұ Щ„Ы• ШҰШ§Щ…Ы•ШҜ Щ„ЫҺШҜЫ•ШҜШұЫҺЩҶ
title_en: ШіЫҺ ШЁЫҢШұЫҢ ЩҲШІЫ•ЫҢ ЪҜЫ•ШұЩ…ЫҢЫҢ ЪҳЫҺШұШІЫ•ЩҲЫҢ ШЁЫ• ЩӮЩҲЩҲЪөШ§ЫҢЫҢ 900 Щ…Ы•ШӘШұ Щ„Ы• ШҰШ§Щ…Ы•ШҜ Щ„ЫҺШҜЫ•ШҜШұЫҺЩҶ
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ Щ„Ы•ЩҶЫҺЩҲ Ш®Ш§ЩҶЩҲЩҲЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҢ Ъ©ШұЫҺЩҶШҙЫҢЩҶЫҢ ЩӮЫ•ШІШ§ЫҢ ЩҫЫҺЩҶШ¬ЩҲЫҺЩҶ ШҜШ§ШҢ Щ…ЩҶШҜШ§ЪөЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШӘЫ•Щ…Ы•ЩҶ 10 ШіШ§Ъө ШЁЫ• Ш¬Ы•ШіШӘЫ•ЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҢ Щ…Ш§ЩҶШҜЩҲЩҲШҢ ШЁЫ•ЪөШ§Щ… ШЁЫ• ШҰЫҢШұШ§ШҜЫ•ЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҢ ЩҫЫҶЪөШ§ЫҢЫҢЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ• Щ…Щ„Щ…Щ„Ш§ЩҶЫҺ Щ„Ы•ЪҜЫ•Ъө ЩҶЫ•Ш®ЫҶШҙЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҢ ШҜЫ•ЪҜЩ…Ы•ЩҶ ШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШӘ. Щ…ШӯЫ•Щ…Щ…Ы•ШҜ ЩҮШ§ЩҲЪ©Ш§Шұ ШӘЩҲЩҲШҙШЁЩҲЩҲЫҢ ЩҶЫ•Ш®ЫҶШҙЫҢЫҢ ЩҫЩҲЩҲЪ©Ш§ЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ШҜЫ•Щ…Ш§Шұ ЩҲ Щ…Ш§ШіЩҲЩ„Ъ©Ы•ЫҢЫ•ШҢ ШЁЫ•ЪөШ§Щ… ЩҶЫ•Ш®ЫҶШҙЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ы•ЫҢ ЩҶЫ•Ъ© ЩҮЫ•Шұ ШұЫҺЪҜШұЫҢ Щ„Ы• Ш®Ы•ЩҲЩҶЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҶЫ•Ъ©ШұШҜЩҲЩҲЫ•ШҢ ШЁЫ•ЪөЪ©ЩҲ Щ„Ы• ЩӮЩҲШӘШ§ШЁШ®Ш§ЩҶЫ•ШҙШҜШ§ ЩҶЩ…ЩҲЩҲЩҶЫ•ЫҢ ШІЫҢШұЫ•Ъ©ЫҢЫҢЫ•

title: ЪҜЩҲШ§ШұШҜЫҢЫҶЩ„Ш§ ЩҲ ШҜЩҲШ§ЫҢЫҢЩҶ ЩҮЫ•ЩҶШ§ШіЫ• ШЁЫҶ ШұЪ©Ш§ШЁЫ•ШұЫҢЪ©ШұШҜЩҶ Щ„Ы•ШіЫ•Шұ ШЁШұШҜЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЩҫШұЫҺЩ…Ы•ШұЩ„ЫҢЪҜ
title_en: ЪҜЩҲШ§ШұШҜЫҢЫҶЩ„Ш§ ЩҲ ШҜЩҲШ§ЫҢЫҢЩҶ ЩҮЫ•ЩҶШ§ШіЫ• ШЁЫҶ ШұЪ©Ш§ШЁЫ•ШұЫҢЪ©ШұШҜЩҶ Щ„Ы•ШіЫ•Шұ ШЁШұШҜЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЩҫШұЫҺЩ…Ы•ШұЩ„ЫҢЪҜ
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ

title: ЪҶЫ•ЩҶШҜ ЩҮЫҶЪ©Ш§ШұЫҺЪ© ШұЫҺЪҜШұЩҶ Щ„Ы• ЪҜЫ•Ъ•Ш§ЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЩҶЫ•ЫҢЩ…Ш§Шұ ШЁЫҶ Щ…ЫҶЩҶШҜЫҢШ§Щ„
title_en: ЪҶЫ•ЩҶШҜ ЩҮЫҶЪ©Ш§ШұЫҺЪ© ШұЫҺЪҜШұЩҶ Щ„Ы• ЪҜЫ•Ъ•Ш§ЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЩҶЫ•ЫҢЩ…Ш§Шұ ШЁЫҶ Щ…ЫҶЩҶШҜЫҢШ§Щ„
summary: ШұЩҲЩҲШҜШ§ЩҲ ШҜЫҢШ¬ЫҢШӘШ§Ъө ШіШӘШ§ЩҒЫҢ ШӘЫ•Ъ©ЩҶЫҢЪ©ЫҢЫҢ ЩҮЫ•ЪөШЁЪҳШ§ШұШҜЫ•ЫҢ ШЁЫ•ШұШ§ШІЫҢЩ„ ЪҜЩҲЩ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШҰШ§Щ…Ш§ШҜЫ•ЫҢЫҢ ЩҶЫ•ЫҢЩ…Ш§Шұ ШҜШ§ШіЫҺЩ„ЪӨШ§ ЩҮЫ•ЫҢЫ• ШЁЫҶ Щ…ЫҶЩҶШҜЫҢШ§Щ„ ЩҲ ЪҶЫ•ЩҶШҜ ЩҮЫҶЪ©Ш§ШұЫҺЪ©ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЩҶЫҢШҙШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҲЩҲЫ•ШҢ ШҜЫ•ЪөЫҺЩҶ ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ЩҮЫҶЪ©Ш§ШұШ§ЩҶЫ• ШұЫҺЪҜШұЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШЁШ§ЩҶЪҜЩҮЫҺШҙШӘЪ©ШұШҜЩҶЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢШІШ§ЩҶЫ• ШЁЫҶ Щ…ЫҶЩҶШҜЫҢШ§Щ„. ЩҮЫ•ЪөШЁЪҳШ§ШұШҜЫ•ЫҢ ШЁЫ•ШұШ§ШІЫҢЩ„ Щ„Ы•ШЁЫ•ШұШҜЫ•Щ… ШЁЪ•ЫҢШ§ШұЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ЪҶШ§ШұЫ•ЩҶЩҲЩҲШіШіШ§ШІШҜШ§ЫҢЫ• ШіЫ•ШЁШ§ШұЫ•ШӘ ШЁЫ• ЩҶЫ•ЫҢЩ…Ш§ШұШҢ ЪҶЩҲЩҶЪ©Ы• ШіШӘШ§ЩҒЫҢ ШұШ§ЩҮЫҺЩҶЫ•ШұШ§ЩҶ ЪҶЫ•ЩҶШҜ Ш®Ш§ЪөЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ЩҶЫ•ШұЫҺЩҶЫҢЫҢШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ы•ШіЫ•Шұ ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢШІШ§ЩҶЫ• Ъ©ЫҶЪ©ШұШҜЩҲЩҲЫ•ШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ•. ЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ы•Щ… ЩҶЫҢЪҜЫ•ШұШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢЫ• ЩҶЫ•ЫҢЩ…Ш§Шұ Щ„Ы•Щ… ШҜЩҲШ§ЫҢЫҢШ§ЩҶЫ•ШҜШ§ ЩҶЫ•ЫҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЫҢЩҲЫ• 8 ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢ Щ„Ы•ШіЫ•ШұЫҢЫ•Ъ© ШҰЫ•ЩҶШ¬Ш§Щ… ШЁШҜШ§ШӘШҢ ШҰЫ•Щ…Ы•Шҙ ЩҫЫҺЩҲЫ•ШұЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШіЫ•ШұЫ•Ъ©ЫҢЫҢЫ• ШЁЫҶ ШҰШ§Щ…Ш§ШҜЫ•ЫҢЫҢ Щ„Ы• Щ…ЫҶЩҶШҜЫҢШ§Щ„. ШіЫ•ШұЫ•Ъ•Ш§ЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲЫ•ШҢ ШҰШ§Щ…Ш§ШҜЫ•ЩҶЫ•ШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶЫҢ ЩҶЫ•ЫҢЩ…Ш§Шұ Щ„Ы• ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢЫҢ ШЁЫ•ШұШ§Щ…ШЁЫ•Шұ Щ…ЫҢШұШ§ШіЫҶЪө ШЁЫ•ШЁЫҺ ЩҫШ§ШіШ§ЩҲШҢ ЩҶЫҢЪҜЫ•ШұШ§ЩҶЫҢЫҢ ШІЫҶШұЫҢ Щ„Ш§ЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұЩ„ЫҶ ШҰШ§ЩҶЪҶЫҺЩ„ЫҶШӘЫҢ ШҜШұЩҲШіШӘЪ©ШұШҜЩҲЩҲЫ•ШҢ Щ„Ы• Ъ©Ш§ШӘЫҺЪ©ШҜШ§ ЩҫЫҺШҙШӘШұ ШұШ§ЩҮЫҺЩҶЫ•Шұ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢШІШ§ЩҶЫ•Ъ©Ы• ЩҲ ЫҢШ§ЩҶЫ•ЫҢ ШіШ§ЩҶШӘЫҶШіЫҢ ШҰШ§ЪҜШ§ШҜШ§Шұ Ъ©ШұШҜШЁЩҲЩҲЫ•ЩҲЫ• Ъ©Ы• ШҜЫ•ШЁЫҺШӘ Щ„Ы• ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШҰШ§Щ…Ш§ШҜЫ•ШЁЫҺШӘ. Ш®Ш§ЪөЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШҜЫҢЪ©Ы•ЫҢ ЩҶЫ•ШұЫҺЩҶЫҢШҢ ШЁЪ•ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢ ЩҶЫ•ЫҢЩ…Ш§Шұ ШЁЩҲЩҲ ШЁЫҶ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢ ЩҶЫ•Ъ©ШұШҜЩҶ Щ„Ы• Ъ©Щ„Ш§ШіЫҢЪ©ЫҶ ШЁЫ•ШұШ§Щ…ШЁЫ•Шұ ЩҫШ§ЪөЩ…ЫҺШұШ§ШіШҢ ШӘЫ•ЩҶЫҢШ§ Щ„Ы•ШЁЫ•Шұ ШҰЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢЪҜЫ•Ъ©Ы• ЪҶЫҢЩ…Ы•ЩҶЫҢ ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЪ©ШұШҜ ШЁЩҲЩҲ. ШіШӘШ§ЩҒЫҢ ЩҮЫ•ЪөШЁЪҳШ§ШұШҜЫ•ЫҢ ШЁЫ•ШұШ§ШІЫҢЩ„ ШҜЫ•ЪөЫҺЩҶ "ЩҫЫҺЩҲЫҢШіШӘЫ• ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢШІШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫҶ ЩҮЫ•Щ…ЩҲЩҲ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ© ШҰШ§Щ…Ш§ШҜЫ•ШЁЫҺШӘШҢ ЩҶЫ•Ъ© Ш®ЫҶЫҢ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ЩҮЫ•ЪөШЁЪҳЫҺШұЫҺШӘ". ШҰЫ•Щ…Ы• ЩҲШ§ЫҢЪ©ШұШҜЩҲЩҲЫ• ЪҜЩҲЩ…Ш§ЩҶ Щ„Ы•ШіЫ•Шұ ШЁШ§ЩҶЪҜЩҮЫҺШҙШӘЪ©ШұШҜЩҶЫҢ ЩҶЫ•ЫҢЩ…Ш§Шұ ШЁЫҶ Щ…ЫҶЩҶШҜЫҢШ§Щ„ ШҜШұЩҲШіШӘШЁЪ©Ш§ШӘ. ЩҶЫ•ЫҢЩ…Ш§Шұ ШіШ§ЪөШ§ЩҶЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШІЫҶШұЫ• ШіЫ•ШұЪ©ШұШҜШ§ЫҢЫ•ШӘЫҢ ЩҮЫҺЪөЫҢ ЩҮЫҺШұШҙШЁЫ•ШұЫҢ ШЁЫ•ШұШ§ШІЫҢЩ„ ШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШӘШҢ ШЁЫ•ЪөШ§Щ… ЩҫЫҺЪ©Ш§ЩҶ ЩҲ ШҜЩҲЩҲШұЪ©Ы•ЩҲШӘЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ Щ„Ы• ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢЪҜШ§Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ Ъ©Ш§ШұЫҢЪҜЫ•ШұЫҢЫҢ ЪҜЫ•ЩҲШұЫ•ЫҢ Щ„Ы•ШіЫ•Шұ ШҰШ§ШіШӘЫҢ ШҜШұЩҲШіШӘ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҲЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ШұШ§ЩҮЫҺЩҶЫ•ШұШ§ЩҶЫҢШҙ ШҜЫ•ЫҢШ§ЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫҺШӘ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢШІШ§ЩҶЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШӘЫ•ЩҲШ§ЩҲ ШҰШ§Щ…Ш§ШҜЫ• ШЁШЁЫ•ЩҶЫ• Щ…ЫҶЩҶШҜЫҢШ§Щ„. ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ

title: Three Kurdistan villages to be nominated for UN Best Tourism Villages title
title_en: Three Kurdistan villages to be nominated for UN Best Tourism Villages title
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - Seven rural tourist villages from across Iraq, including three in the Kurdistan Region, are set to compete for the United NationsвҖҷ Best Tourism Villages title, a senior tourism official in Baghdad told Rudaw on Tuesday, as part of efforts to boost the countryвҖҷs tourism profile. вҖңSeven villages from Iraq will be nominated and submitted to the UN Tourism organization to compete for the title of Best Tourism Villages, three of which are located in the Kurdistan Region,вҖқ said Ali Yassin, spokesperson for IraqвҖҷs Tourism Authority. Yassin detailed that the nominated villages in the Kurdistan Region are Biyara in the eastern Halabja province, Hiror in the northern Duhok province, and Rawanduz in Erbil province. The remaining villages are distributed across IraqвҖҷs southern provinces of Dhi Qar, Karbala, and Wasit, as well as the northern province of Nineveh. According to the tourism official, вҖңseven comprehensive nomination dossiers for the villages will be submitted to UN Tourism for evaluation by a team of experts, who will select one or more villages based on precise criteria, including the quality of tourism services, environmental and natural preservation, the promotion of green tourism, and the involvement of local communities in tourism development and integration.вҖқ Yassin noted that the move is expected to have a significant impact on revitalizing the tourism sector in Iraq and the Kurdistan Region in general. The decision on the worldвҖҷs Best Tourism Villages is expected to be announced in early 2027. Of note, the Kurdistan Region is geographically characterized by predominantly mountainous terrain, offering particularly striking natural landscapes in its northern and northeastern areas, especially along the borders with Turkey and Iran. In addition to its breathtaking natural scenery, the Region is rich in cultural and historical heritage, positioning it as a significant part of ancient Mesopotamia. title: ШЁШ§ЪөЫҢЫҶШІЫҢ ЩҶЩҲЫҺЫҢ ЪӨШ§ШӘЫҢЪ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЫҶ Щ…Ы•ШіШұЩҲШұ ШЁШ§ШұШІШ§ЩҶЫҢ: Ъ©ЩҲШұШҜШіШӘШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШҜЪөЫҢ ЩҫШ§ЩҫШ§ШҜШ§ЫҢЫ•
title_en: ШЁШ§ЪөЫҢЫҶШІЫҢ ЩҶЩҲЫҺЫҢ ЪӨШ§ШӘЫҢЪ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЫҶ Щ…Ы•ШіШұЩҲШұ ШЁШ§ШұШІШ§ЩҶЫҢ: Ъ©ЩҲШұШҜШіШӘШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШҜЪөЫҢ ЩҫШ§ЩҫШ§ШҜШ§ЫҢЫ•
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ ШҰЫҺЩҲШ§ШұЫ•ЫҢ ШҜЩҲЫҺЩҶЫҺ (Ъ©ЫҶЪ•ШЁЫ•ЩҶШҜЫҢ ШҰШ§ШҙШӘЫҢЫҢ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…Ы•ЪөШ§ЫҢЫ•ШӘЫҢ ЩҲ ШҰШ§ШІШ§ШҜЫҢ) ШЁЫ• ШЁЫ•ШҙШҜШ§ШұЫҢЫҢ ЪҳЩ…Ш§ШұЫ•ЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҢ ШЁЫ•ШұЪҶШ§ЩҲ Щ„Ы• Ъ©Ы•ШіШ§ЫҢЫ•ШӘЫҢЫҢЫ• ШіЫҢШ§ШіЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶШҢ ШҙШ§ЩҶШҜЫ• ШӯЪ©ЩҲЩ…ЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҮЫ•ШұЫҺЩ…ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҲШұШҜШіШӘШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ ЩҶЩҲЫҺЩҶЫ•ШұШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШұЫҺЪ©Ш®ШұШ§ЩҲЫ• Щ…Ы•ШҜЫ•ЩҶЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЫҢ ЩҫЫҺЪ©ШұШҜ

title: KRG urges Baghdad to finalize stalled customs unification deal
title_en: KRG urges Baghdad to finalize stalled customs unification deal
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - The Kurdistan Regional Government (KRG) has formally petitioned the Iraqi federal government to convene an urgent session of the Ministerial Council for the Economy to finalize a customs and trade unification agreement that has remained stalled for more than a month, a senior commerce official in Erbil told Rudaw on Tuesday. Erbil and Baghdad reached an agreement in early April to implement the United Nations-backed Automated System for Customs Data (ASYCUDA) across the Kurdistan RegionвҖҷs border crossings, aiming to unify customs procedures and modernize trade infrastructure between the two sides. However, Gilan Haji Said, head of ErbilвҖҷs Chamber of Commerce and Industry, told Rudaw that вҖңso far, the Ministerial Council for Economy has not held the meeting designated to ratify this understanding.вҖқ In mid-April, the KRG submitted an official proposal to the Iraqi cabinet requesting a meeting of the council to resolve outstanding issues and complete the requirements for implementing the agreement вҖңwithout delay.вҖқ The proposal included several key demands intended to balance federal oversight with the RegionвҖҷs administrative particularities. The demands include adopting bilingual customs tariffs in Kurdish and Arabic, granting Kurdish traders access to US dollars at the official exchange rate set by the Central Bank of Iraq (CBI), assigning a joint committee to conduct field visits to unrecognized border crossings in the Kurdistan Region to facilitate their formal legal integration, and mutually recognizing trade identities and tax clearance certificates issued by either the KRG or the federal government. However, the lack of formal approval from the federal council has delayed implementation of the framework, which seeks to streamline trade and resolve the legal status of regional companies. summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ 11ЩҖЫ•Щ…ЫҢЩҶ ЩҒЫҺШіШӘЫҢЪӨШ§ЪөЫҢ ЩҶЫҺЩҲШҜЫ•ЩҲЪөЫ•ШӘЫҢЫҢ ШҙШ§ЩҶЫҶЫҢ ШҰШ§Щ…Ы•ШҜ ШЁЫ• ШҜШұЩҲШҙЩ…ЫҢ "ШҜЫҢШ§Щ„ЫҶЪҜ ШЁЫҶ ШҰШ§ШҙШӘЫҢ" ЩҲ ШЁЫ• ШұЫҺЩҲЪ•Ы•ШіЩ…ЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШӘШ§ЫҢШЁЫ•ШӘ ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЫҢ ЩҫЫҺ Ъ©ШұШҜ ЩҲ ШұЫҺЩҲЪ•Ы•ШіЩ…ЫҢ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЩҒЫҺШіШӘЫҢЪӨШ§ЪөЫ•Ъ©Ы• Щ„Ы• Щ…ЫҶШІЫ•Ш®Ш§ЩҶЫ•ЫҢ ШҙШ§ШұЫҢ ЩӮЫ•ШіШұЫҢ Ш¬Ы•Щ…ЫҢЩ„ ЩҫШ§ШҙШ§ ШЁЫ•Ъ•ЫҺЩҲЫ• ЪҶЩҲЩҲ. ШӘЫҺЫҢШҜШ§ ЩҮЩҲЩҶЫ•ШұЩ…Ы•ЩҶШҜШ§ЩҶШҢ ШҙШ§ЩҶЫҶЪ©Ш§ШұШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ ЪҳЩ…Ш§ШұЫ•ЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҢ ШІЫҶШұ Щ„Ы• Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫ• Ш¬Щ„ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҲШұШҜЫҢЫҢЫ•ЩҲЫ• ШЁЫ•ШҙШҜШ§ШұЫҢЫҢШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШұЫҺЩҲЪ•Ы•ШіЩ…Ы•Ъ©Ы• ШҜШ§ Ъ©ШұШҜ ЩҲ ЩҫЫ•ЫҢШ§Щ…ЫҢ ШҰШ§ШҙШӘЫҢЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШҜШ§.

title: Damascus vets thousands of teachers in Hasaka amid integration efforts
title_en: Damascus vets thousands of teachers in Hasaka amid integration efforts
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - SyriaвҖҷs education ministry said on Tuesday that it is making headway in reviewing the academic and professional credentials of teachers in the Kurdish-majority Hasaka province, as part of broader efforts to вҖңregularize the status of employeesвҖқ in northeast Syria (Rojava) and implement the January integration agreement between Damascus and the Kurdish-led enclave. A committee appointed by the ministry is вҖңreviewing the files of teachers from the Hasaka Education DirectorateвҖқ who have been performing their duties since the establishment of the Kurdish-led Democratic Autonomous Administration of North and East Syria (DAANES), the Syrian education ministry said in a statement. вҖңThe committee is focusing on reviewing documents, certificates, and employee records to ensure they meet the required standards,вҖқ the statement added, noting the Hasaka Education DirectorateвҖҷs commitment to вҖңproviding full logistical and technical support to facilitate the committeeвҖҷs work and ensuring the vetting is completed accurately and carefully within the specified timelines.вҖқ The ministry further stated that the effort is essential to вҖңregularize the status of employees [in Rojava] and complete the integration processвҖҰ in what would ensure the highest levels of transparency and administrative fairness in the education sector.вҖқ Damascus and the Kurdish-led Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) - which operates as the de facto army in Rojava - reached a landmark agreement on January 29, with significant mediation from the United States through its Special Envoy for Syria, Tom Barrack, as well as Kurdish political figures from the Kurdistan Region. The 14-point agreement establishes a permanent and comprehensive ceasefire and calls for the integration of RojavaвҖҷs civil and military institutions under Syrian state control. The two sides have since been holding talks to implement the deal, with education emerging as a central issue in the negotiations. The Syrian government

title: Kurdish-language education in Rojava produces fluent new generation: Academic
title_en: Kurdish-language education in Rojava produces fluent new generation: Academic
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - A senior official at University of Rojava said Kurdish-language education in northeast Syria (Rojava) has produced a new generation of fluent Kurdish speakers after nearly 15 years of mother-tongue instruction, amid ongoing debates with Damascus over the future of the regionвҖҷs education system. вҖңChildren in Rojava now speak Kurdish very fluently. There is a major difference between the language of our children and that of our generation,вҖқ Zina Ali, co-chair of the University of Rojava, told Rudaw's Nalin Hassan on Sunday. вҖңToday, our children are teaching us the language because they are educated in their mother tongue at school.вҖқ Kurdish-language education was introduced in Rojava after Kurdish authorities established autonomous institutions during the Syrian civil war. Schools under the Autonomous Administration currently teach most subjects in Kurdish, Arabic, and Syriac, depending on the community and region. The future of the system, however, remains uncertain as negotiations continue between Damascus and the Kurdish-led Autonomous Administration following a landmark agreement signed late January between the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) and the Syrian interim government to integrate civilian and military institutions. Ali said Damascus had proposed limiting Kurdish instruction in schools to only two classes per week, a move rejected by the Autonomous AdministrationвҖҷs education authorities. вҖңBecause of this disagreement, there has been discussion about translating the state curricula into Kurdish,вҖқ she said, stressing that no final decision has yet been made. The Kurdish official noted that the Autonomous AdministrationвҖҷs current curriculum is expected to remain in place at least until next year while talks continue. Ali also emphasized that the University of Rojava intends to preserve its Kurdish identity regardless of any future arrangement with the Syrian government. вҖңThe main characteristic of the University of Rojava is the Kurdi

title: ЩҮШ§Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ЩҒЫҢШҜШ§ЩҶ: ШҰЫ•Щ…ШұЫҢЪ©Ш§ ЩҲ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫҢШіШӘЫҢ ЩҫЫҺЩҲЫҢШіШӘЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫҶ Ъ©ЫҶШӘШ§ЫҢЫҢЩҮЫҺЩҶШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫ• Ш¬Ы•ЩҶЪҜ ЩҮЫ•ЫҢЫ•
title_en: ЩҮШ§Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ЩҒЫҢШҜШ§ЩҶ: ШҰЫ•Щ…ШұЫҢЪ©Ш§ ЩҲ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫҢШіШӘЫҢ ЩҫЫҺЩҲЫҢШіШӘЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫҶ Ъ©ЫҶШӘШ§ЫҢЫҢЩҮЫҺЩҶШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫ• Ш¬Ы•ЩҶЪҜ ЩҮЫ•ЫҢЫ•
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ ШЁЫ•ЪҜЩҲЫҺШұЫ•ЫҢ ШҜЩҲШ§ЫҢЫҢЩҶ ШҰШ§Щ…Ш§ШұЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ШҜШ§Щ…Ы•ШІШұШ§ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ШҰШ§Щ…Ш§ШұЫҢ ШӘЩҲШұЪ©ЫҢШ§ (TГңД°K)ШҢ ЩӮЫ•ШЁШ§ШұЫ•ЫҢ Ш®ЫҺШІШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШӘЩҲШұЪ©ЫҢШ§ ЩҲ ШЁШ§Ъ©ЩҲЩҲШұЫҢ Ъ©ЩҲШұШҜШіШӘШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫ•ШұЫ•ЩҲ ШЁЪҶЩҲЩҲЪ©ШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ• ШҜЫ•ЪҶЫҺШӘ. ЩҫШ§ШұЫҺШІЪҜШ§ЫҢ ШҙШұЩҶЫ•Ш® ШЁЫ•ШұШІШӘШұЫҢЩҶ ЩҲ ШҜЫҺШұШіЩ… ЩҶШІЩ…ШӘШұЫҢЩҶ ШӘЫҺЪ©Ъ•Ш§ЫҢ ЩӮЫ•ШЁШ§ШұЫ•ЫҢ Ш®ЫҺШІШ§ЩҶЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШӘЫҶЩ…Ш§ШұЪ©ШұШҜЩҲЩҲЫ•.

title: Kurds continue to protest exclusion of Kurdish from Hasaka signboard
title_en: Kurds continue to protest exclusion of Kurdish from Hasaka signboard
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - The Syrian governmentвҖҷs insistence on excluding Kurdish from the signboard of the Justice Palace in the Kurdish-majority city of Hasaka has sparked days of protests, leaving the building without a signboard amid tensions over language rights and the integration process between Damascus and the Kurdish authorities in northeast Syria (Rojava). Kurds have gathered outside the Justice Palace for several consecutive days, demanding that Kurdish be included alongside Arabic on the buildingвҖҷs signboard. Protesters waved Kurdistan flags, chanted Kurdish slogans and accused Damascus of ignoring commitments made under recent agreements with the Kurdish-led administration. вҖңWe want the Kurdish language [on the signboard]. Kurdish is our language and we will not accept any other language,вҖқ protester Suhayla Khisho told Rudaw on Monday. The controversy began after Syrian authorities removed a bilingual Kurdish-Arabic signboard from the Justice Palace and replaced it with one written in Arabic and English. The new signboard was later torn down by angry protesters. Damascus then installed another signboard written only in Arabic, which was also removed, leaving the building without any signboard. The dispute comes as the Kurdish authorities in Rojava move to integrate their civilian and military institutions into Syrian state structures under a landmark January 29 agreement reached between the Kurdish-led Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) and Damascus following weeks of clashes. The agreement includes provisions related to Kurdish language rights and broader Kurdish participation in state institutions. вҖңIf the integration proceeds well, we will support it. We will live with the tribes of the region as brothers, provided that our rights are guaranteed. The January 29 agreement between [SDF chief] Mazloum Abdi and [Syrian president] Ahmed al-Sharaa stipulates that Kurdish is the second language in Syria as a whole,вҖқ protester Idris Mohammed told Rudaw. title: Chinese company says Iraq power plants to add 1200 MW by summer
title_en: Chinese company says Iraq power plants to add 1200 MW by summer
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - A Chinese state-owned company operating in Iraq said Monday that several power plants under construction are expected to come online in summer, adding more than 1,200 megawatts to IraqвҖҷs national grid as the country struggles to meet rising power demand. вҖңThe company is currently building gas-fired power plants that will enter service next summer and will add around 1,000 megawatts to the national grid,вҖқ Ahmed al-Amin, regional director of Shanghai Electric, told Iraqi state media. He added that the company is also upgrading the al-Haritha station in southern Basra province and the Wasit station in eastern Wasit province. Haritha station is expected to add about 220 megawatts, while Wasit is projected to add around 2,520 megawatts. Iraqi electricity ministry spokesperson Ahmed Mousa told Rudaw on Sunday that Iraq expects a generation deficit of around 42,000 megawatts during the summer, with overall demand projected to reach 60,000 megawatts. He also revealed the federal governmentвҖҷs plan to purchase power from the Kurdistan Region to meet the demand after it was halted due to the Iran war since February 28. Electricity generation in Iraq frequently falls short during peak summer and winter seasons. The shortage has worsened since Washington rescinded IraqвҖҷs waiver to import electricity from Iran in March 2025 as part of the Trump administrationвҖҷs вҖңmaximum pressureвҖқ campaign against Tehran. The electricity ministry said in late April that power grid interconnection projects with neighboring countries are expected to become operational before the summer season, with projected imports of around 600 megawatts from Turkey, 500 megawatts from Gulf countries in the first phase, and 150 megawatts from Jordan. title: Two ships carrying Iraqi oil passed Strait of Hormuz in May: Ministry
title_en: Two ships carrying Iraqi oil passed Strait of Hormuz in May: Ministry
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - IraqвҖҷs oil ministry said Tuesday that two tankers carrying Iraqi crude oil have passed through the Strait of Hormuz this month amid ongoing maritime restrictions in the strategic waterway. "This month, two ships carrying Iraqi oil passed through the Strait of Hormuz. The last one loaded one million barrels of oil from Basra on Sunday,вҖқ Iraq's Oil Ministry undersecretary Basim Khudair told Rudaw. The passage comes after months of maritime navigation restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz following an Iranian-imposed blockade after the US and Israel launched a wide-scale aerial campaign against Iran on February 28. The conflict lasted more than six weeks before the sides agreed to a Pakistan-mediated ceasefire on April 8, paving the way for negotiations. Khudair said that if the waterway reopens вҖңwithin a week, they can reach the export capacity of three million barrels." Iranian state-affiliated media announced Monday that a tanker carrying Iraqi crude oil had passed through the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday. The Maltese-flagged tanker departed IraqвҖҷs southern Basra port in late April and is expected to arrive at VietnamвҖҷs Nghi Son port on May 26, according to vessel tracking service MarineTraffic. In early April, Ebrahim Zolfaghari, spokesperson for the IRGCвҖҷs Khatam al-Anbiya Headquarters, said that "brotherly Iraq" would be exempt from the blockade, which he said would apply only to "hostile nations." Despite that, Iraq has struggled to restore oil exports to prewar levels because it relies heavily on foreign vessels for crude shipments. Before the six-week war, Iraq produced around 4.5 million barrels of oil per day and exported roughly 3.5 million barrels daily, with nearly 90 percent of exports passing through the Strait of Hormuz. According to Iraqi oil ministry figures, exports dropped to 18.6 million barrels in March, generating about $1.96 billion in revenue, compared with more than 99 million barrels and $6.81 billion in February. title: Kuwait says IRGC members arrested after attempted infiltration
title_en: Kuwait says IRGC members arrested after attempted infiltration
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - Kuwait said Tuesday that four members of IranвҖҷs Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) were arrested in early May after allegedly attempting to infiltrate Kuwaiti territory, leaving one Kuwaiti security personnel injured. KuwaitвҖҷs foreign ministry expressed вҖңstrong condemnationвҖқ of вҖңan armed group from elementsвҖқ of the IRGC вҖңinfiltrating Bubiyan Island with the aim of carrying out hostile acts against the State of Kuwait, and their engagement with Kuwaiti Armed Forces personnel before being apprehended.вҖқ It added that a member of the Kuwaiti armed forces was injured in the confrontation. The four men said they had been tasked by Iran's IRGC with вҖңinfiltratingвҖқ Bubiyan Island, Kuwait's interior ministry said in a statement published by state news agency KUNA. Bubiyan is KuwaitвҖҷs largest island and is located near the Iranian coast. According to the interior ministry, the group attempted to enter the island on May 1 вҖңaboard a fishing boat specially chartered to carry out hostile actions against Kuwait.вҖқ KuwaitвҖҷs foreign ministry said Tehran вҖңbears full responsibility for these hostile acts, and that the State of Kuwait reserves its full and inherent right to self-defense.вҖқ The ministry also called on Tehran to вҖңimmediately and unconditionally cease its unlawful hostile acts that threaten the security and stability of the region and undermine regional and international efforts aimed at de-escalation.вҖқ Rudaw moderates all comments submitted on our website. title: ЩҲЫ•ШІШ§ШұЫ•ШӘЫҢ ЩҶШ§ЩҲШ®ЫҶЫҢ Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮ ШЁЫҶ ШұЩҲЩҲШҜШ§ЩҲ: ШІЫҢШ§ШӘШұ Щ„Ы• ШҜЩҲЩҲ ЩҮЫ•ШІШ§Шұ ШЁЫҢШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶЫ•ШӘЫ• Ш®Ш§ЩҲЫ•ЩҶЫҢ ЩҲЪөШ§ШӘЫҢЩҶШ§Щ…Ы•ЫҢ Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮЫҢ
title_en: ЩҲЫ•ШІШ§ШұЫ•ШӘЫҢ ЩҶШ§ЩҲШ®ЫҶЫҢ Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮ ШЁЫҶ ШұЩҲЩҲШҜШ§ЩҲ: ШІЫҢШ§ШӘШұ Щ„Ы• ШҜЩҲЩҲ ЩҮЫ•ШІШ§Шұ ШЁЫҢШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶЫ•ШӘЫ• Ш®Ш§ЩҲЫ•ЩҶЫҢ ЩҲЪөШ§ШӘЫҢЩҶШ§Щ…Ы•ЫҢ Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮЫҢ
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ ЩҶШ§Ъ©ЫҶЪ©ЫҢ Щ„Ы•ШіЫ•Шұ ШҜШ§ШЁЫ•ШҙЪ©ШұШҜЩҶЫҢ ЩҲЫ•ШІШ§ШұЫ•ШӘЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ЩҲ ШЁЫ•ШұШЁЪҳЫҺШұЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶШҢ ЩҮЫҺШҙШӘШ§ Щ„Ы•ЩҶЫҺЩҲ ЩҮШ§ЩҲЩҫЫ•ЫҢЩ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢЫҢ ЪҶЩҲШ§ШұЪҶЫҺЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЩҮШ§ЩҲШҰШ§ЩҮЫ•ЩҶЪҜЫҢ ЫҢЫ•Ъ©Щ„Ш§ ЩҶЫ•ШЁЩҲЩҲЫ•ШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ Ш№Ы•Щ„Щү ЩҒШ§Щ„Шӯ ШІЫ•ЫҢШҜЩү Щ…Ы•ШұШ¬Щү ЩҮЫ•ЫҢЫ•ШҢ ШЁЪ•ЫҢШ§ШұЫ• ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ЩҮШ§ЩҲЩҫЫ•ЫҢЩ…Ш§ЩҶЫҺШӘЫҢЫҢЫ• ШЁЫҶ ШӘЫҺЩҫЫ•Ъ•Ш§ЩҶШҜЩҶЩү ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ШҜЫҶШ®Ы• Ъ©ЫҶШЁШЁЫҺШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ•. ЪҜЩҲШӘЫ•ШЁЫҺЪҳЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҶШ¬ЩҲЩҲЩ…Ы•ЩҶЩү ШЁШ§ЪөШ§Щү ШҰЫҢШіЩ„Ш§Щ…Щү ШҜЫ•ЪөЫҺШӘ: "ШЁЫ• ЩҫШҙШӘЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶЫҢЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШ§ШҢ ЩҫЫҺЩҶШ¬ШҙЫ•Щ…Щ…Ы• ШҜЫ•ЩҶЪҜ ШЁЫ• Ъ©Ш§ШЁЫҢЩҶЫ•ЫҢ ШӯЪ©ЩҲЩ…Ы•ШӘ ШҜЫ•ШҜШұЫҺШӘШҢ"  ШЁЫ•ШұЩҫШұШіЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШҜЫҢЪ©Ы•ЫҢ Щ„Ш§ЫҢЫ•ЩҶЫ• ШҙЫҢШ№Ы•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ЩҫЫҺШҙШЁЫҢЩҶЫҢ ШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШӘ ШЁЫ• ШҜЩҲЩҲ ЩӮЫҶЩҶШ§Ш® ШҜЫ•ЩҶЪҜ ШЁЫ•ЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ШЁЫҢЩҶЫ•ЫҢЫ• ШЁШҜШұЫҺШӘ.

title: ЩҲШұШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШұЫҢЫҢ ЪҜЫҢШ§ЩҶЩ„Ы•ШҜЫ•ШіШӘШҜШ§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҮШ§ЩҲЩҶЫҢШҙШӘЩ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ© Щ„Ы• Ъ©Ы•Щ„Ш§Шұ Щ„Ы• ЩҶЫҶШұЫ•ЫҢ ЪҜШ§ШІЫҢ Щ…Ш§ЪөШ§ЩҶ ШҜШ§
title_en: ЩҲШұШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШұЫҢЫҢ ЪҜЫҢШ§ЩҶЩ„Ы•ШҜЫ•ШіШӘШҜШ§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҮШ§ЩҲЩҶЫҢШҙШӘЩ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ© Щ„Ы• Ъ©Ы•Щ„Ш§Шұ Щ„Ы• ЩҶЫҶШұЫ•ЫҢ ЪҜШ§ШІЫҢ Щ…Ш§ЪөШ§ЩҶ ШҜШ§
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ

title: ЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҺШӘЫҢЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲШұЩҲЩҲЩҫШ§ Ъ©Ш§Шұ ШЁЫҶ ЩҒШұШ§ЩҲШ§ЩҶЪ©ШұШҜЩҶЫҢ ШҰЫ•ШұЪ©Ы• ШҜЫ•ШұЫҢШ§ЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ШЁЫҶ ШӘЫ•ЩҶЪҜЫ•ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШұЩ…ШІ ШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШӘ
title_en: ЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҺШӘЫҢЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲШұЩҲЩҲЩҫШ§ Ъ©Ш§Шұ ШЁЫҶ ЩҒШұШ§ЩҲШ§ЩҶЪ©ШұШҜЩҶЫҢ ШҰЫ•ШұЪ©Ы• ШҜЫ•ШұЫҢШ§ЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ШЁЫҶ ШӘЫ•ЩҶЪҜЫ•ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШұЩ…ШІ ШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШӘ
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ ШӘЫ•ЩӮЫҢЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҢ ШЁЫ•ЩҮЫҺШІ Щ„Ы• ШЁШ§ШІШ§Ъ•ЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ЩӮЫ•ШұЫ•ШЁШ§ЪөШ® Щ„Ы• ШЁШ§Ъ©ЩҲЩҲШұЫҢ ШұЫҶЪҳШҰШ§ЩҲШ§ЫҢ ЩҫШ§Ъ©ШіШӘШ§ЩҶ ШұЩҲЩҲЫҢШҜШ§ ЩҲ ШЁЫ•ЩҮЫҶЫҢЫ•ЩҲЫ• Щ„Ш§ЩҶЫҢЪ©Ы•Щ… 9 Ъ©Ы•Ші Ъ©ЩҲЪҳШұШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ 35 Ъ©Ы•ШіЫҢ ШҜЫҢЪ©Ы•Шҙ ШЁШұЫҢЩҶШҜШ§Шұ ШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶ. ШӘЫ•ЩӮЫҢЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•Ъ©Ы• ШӘШұШіЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШІЫҶШұЫҢ Щ„Ы•ЩҶШ§ЩҲ Ш®Ы•ЪөЪ©ШҜШ§ ШҜШұЩҲШіШӘ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҲЩҲЫ• ЩҲШҢ Щ…Ы•ШӘШұШіЫҢЫҢ ШіЫ•ШұЩҮЫ•ЪөШҜШ§ЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ШҙЫ•Ъ• ЩҲ ЩҫЫҺЪ©ШҜШ§ШҜШ§ЩҶЫ• ШіЩҶЩҲЩҲШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҶЫҺЩҲШ§ЩҶ ЩҫШ§Ъ©ШіШӘШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ ШҰЫ•ЩҒШәШ§ЩҶШіШӘШ§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҮЫҺЩҶШ§ЩҲЫ•ШӘЫ• ЩҫЫҺШҙЫ•ЩҲЫ•

title: ЩҫЩ„Ш§ЩҶЫҺЪ©ЫҢ "ШӘЫҢШұЫҶШұЫҢШіШӘЫҢ" Щ„Ы• ЩҮШ§Щ…ШЁЫҶШұЪҜЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЪөЩ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢШ§ ЩҫЩҲЩҲЪҶЫ•ЪөЪ©ШұШ§ЫҢЫ•ЩҲЫ•
title_en: ЩҫЩ„Ш§ЩҶЫҺЪ©ЫҢ "ШӘЫҢШұЫҶШұЫҢШіШӘЫҢ" Щ„Ы• ЩҮШ§Щ…ШЁЫҶШұЪҜЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЪөЩ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢШ§ ЩҫЩҲЩҲЪҶЫ•ЪөЪ©ШұШ§ЫҢЫ•ЩҲЫ•
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ ЩҶШ§Ъ©ЫҶЪ©ЫҢ Щ„Ы•ШіЫ•Шұ ШҜШ§ШЁЫ•ШҙЪ©ШұШҜЩҶЫҢ ЩҲЫ•ШІШ§ШұЫ•ШӘЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ЩҲ ШЁЫ•ШұШЁЪҳЫҺШұЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶШҢ ЩҮЫҺШҙШӘШ§ Щ„Ы•ЩҶЫҺЩҲ ЩҮШ§ЩҲЩҫЫ•ЫҢЩ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢЫҢ ЪҶЩҲШ§ШұЪҶЫҺЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЩҮШ§ЩҲШҰШ§ЩҮЫ•ЩҶЪҜЫҢ ЫҢЫ•Ъ©Щ„Ш§ ЩҶЫ•ШЁЩҲЩҲЫ•ШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ Ш№Ы•Щ„Щү ЩҒШ§Щ„Шӯ ШІЫ•ЫҢШҜЩү Щ…Ы•ШұШ¬Щү ЩҮЫ•ЫҢЫ•ШҢ ШЁЪ•ЫҢШ§ШұЫ• ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ЩҮШ§ЩҲЩҫЫ•ЫҢЩ…Ш§ЩҶЫҺШӘЫҢЫҢЫ• ШЁЫҶ ШӘЫҺЩҫЫ•Ъ•Ш§ЩҶШҜЩҶЩү ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ШҜЫҶШ®Ы• Ъ©ЫҶШЁШЁЫҺШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ•. ЪҜЩҲШӘЫ•ШЁЫҺЪҳЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҶШ¬ЩҲЩҲЩ…Ы•ЩҶЩү ШЁШ§ЪөШ§Щү ШҰЫҢШіЩ„Ш§Щ…Щү ШҜЫ•ЪөЫҺШӘ: "ШЁЫ• ЩҫШҙШӘЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶЫҢЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШ§ШҢ ЩҫЫҺЩҶШ¬ШҙЫ•Щ…Щ…Ы• ШҜЫ•ЩҶЪҜ ШЁЫ• Ъ©Ш§ШЁЫҢЩҶЫ•ЫҢ ШӯЪ©ЩҲЩ…Ы•ШӘ ШҜЫ•ШҜШұЫҺШӘШҢ"  ШЁЫ•ШұЩҫШұШіЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШҜЫҢЪ©Ы•ЫҢ Щ„Ш§ЫҢЫ•ЩҶЫ• ШҙЫҢШ№Ы•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ЩҫЫҺШҙШЁЫҢЩҶЫҢ ШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШӘ ШЁЫ• ШҜЩҲЩҲ ЩӮЫҶЩҶШ§Ш® ШҜЫ•ЩҶЪҜ ШЁЫ•ЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ШЁЫҢЩҶЫ•ЫҢЫ• ШЁШҜШұЫҺШӘ.

title: ЩҫЫҺЩҶШӘШ§ЪҜЫҶЩҶ: ШӘЫҺЪҶЩҲЩҲЫҢ Ш¬Ы•ЩҶЪҜЫҢ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫҶ ЩҶШІЫҢЪ©Ы•ЫҢ 29 Щ…Щ„ЫҢШ§Шұ ШҜЫҶЩ„Ш§Шұ ШЁЫ•ШұШІШЁЩҲЩҲЫ•ШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ•
title_en: ЩҫЫҺЩҶШӘШ§ЪҜЫҶЩҶ: ШӘЫҺЪҶЩҲЩҲЫҢ Ш¬Ы•ЩҶЪҜЫҢ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫҶ ЩҶШІЫҢЪ©Ы•ЫҢ 29 Щ…Щ„ЫҢШ§Шұ ШҜЫҶЩ„Ш§Шұ ШЁЫ•ШұШІШЁЩҲЩҲЫ•ШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ•
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ ШӘЫ•ЩӮЫҢЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҢ ШЁЫ•ЩҮЫҺШІ Щ„Ы• ШЁШ§ШІШ§Ъ•ЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ЩӮЫ•ШұЫ•ШЁШ§ЪөШ® Щ„Ы• ШЁШ§Ъ©ЩҲЩҲШұЫҢ ШұЫҶЪҳШҰШ§ЩҲШ§ЫҢ ЩҫШ§Ъ©ШіШӘШ§ЩҶ ШұЩҲЩҲЫҢШҜШ§ ЩҲ ШЁЫ•ЩҮЫҶЫҢЫ•ЩҲЫ• Щ„Ш§ЩҶЫҢЪ©Ы•Щ… 9 Ъ©Ы•Ші Ъ©ЩҲЪҳШұШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ 35 Ъ©Ы•ШіЫҢ ШҜЫҢЪ©Ы•Шҙ ШЁШұЫҢЩҶШҜШ§Шұ ШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶ. ШӘЫ•ЩӮЫҢЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•Ъ©Ы• ШӘШұШіЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШІЫҶШұЫҢ Щ„Ы•ЩҶШ§ЩҲ Ш®Ы•ЪөЪ©ШҜШ§ ШҜШұЩҲШіШӘ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҲЩҲЫ• ЩҲШҢ Щ…Ы•ШӘШұШіЫҢЫҢ ШіЫ•ШұЩҮЫ•ЪөШҜШ§ЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ШҙЫ•Ъ• ЩҲ ЩҫЫҺЪ©ШҜШ§ШҜШ§ЩҶЫ• ШіЩҶЩҲЩҲШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҶЫҺЩҲШ§ЩҶ ЩҫШ§Ъ©ШіШӘШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ ШҰЫ•ЩҒШәШ§ЩҶШіШӘШ§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҮЫҺЩҶШ§ЩҲЫ•ШӘЫ• ЩҫЫҺШҙЫ•ЩҲЫ•

title: ШҙШ§Ш®Ы•ЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ш№Ы•ШЁШҜЩҲЪөЪөШ§: ЪҶЫ•ЩҶШҜ Ш®Ш§ЪөЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ЪҜШұЩҶЪҜЩ…Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЫҶ Ъ©Ш§ШұЩҶШ§Щ…Ы•ЫҢ ШӯЪ©ЩҲЩ…Ы•ШӘЫ•Ъ©Ы•ЫҢ Ш№Ы•Щ„ЫҢ ШІЫҢШҜЫҢ ШІЫҢШ§ШҜ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҲЩҲЫ•
title_en: ШҙШ§Ш®Ы•ЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ш№Ы•ШЁШҜЩҲЪөЪөШ§: ЪҶЫ•ЩҶШҜ Ш®Ш§ЪөЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ЪҜШұЩҶЪҜЩ…Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЫҶ Ъ©Ш§ШұЩҶШ§Щ…Ы•ЫҢ ШӯЪ©ЩҲЩ…Ы•ШӘЫ•Ъ©Ы•ЫҢ Ш№Ы•Щ„ЫҢ ШІЫҢШҜЫҢ ШІЫҢШ§ШҜ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҲЩҲЫ•
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ

title: ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ: ШҰШ§ШҙШӘЫҢЫҢ ШұШ§ШіШӘЫ•ЩӮЫҢЩҶЫ• ШЁЫ• ЩҮЫ•Ъ•Ы•ШҙЫ• ШЁЫ•ШҜЫҢ ЩҶШ§ЫҢЫ•ШӘ

title: UK sanctions 12 Iran-linked entities, individuals over вҖҳhostile activityвҖҷ ties
title_en: UK sanctions 12 Iran-linked entities, individuals over вҖҳhostile activityвҖҷ ties
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - The United Kingdom on Monday added a dozen individuals and groups linked to Iran, accusing them of planning or facilitating вҖңhostile activityвҖқ on behalf of the Iranian government, LondonвҖҷs Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) said in a notice. The notice details that the designations include nine individuals - seven of them of Iranian descent - some of whom are suspected of being вҖңinvolved in hostile activity by the Government of IranвҖқ or by an вҖңarmed group backed by the Government of Iran,вҖқ including вҖңthreatening, planning or conducting attacks against persons and assets in the United Kingdom or any other country.вҖқ Furthermore, some individuals are accused of conduct that вҖңfacilitates (or is intended to facilitate) or gives support or assistanceвҖқ to hostile acts, including вҖңproviding financial services to individuals and entities involved in activity which is intended to cause the destabilisation of the United Kingdom or any other country.вҖқ Meanwhile, the three sanctioned entities include the Zindashti network, which the FCDO said вҖңis or has been involved in hostile activity by an armed group backed by the Government of Iran, including threatening, planning or conducting attacks against persons and assets in the United Kingdom or any other country.вҖқ The other two entities are identified as Berelian Exchange and GCM Exchange, which the Office said are linked to вҖңconduct which facilitates hostile activity by the Government of Iran, namely providing financial services to individuals and entities involved in activity which is intended to cause the destabilisation of the United Kingdom or any other country.вҖқ The UK government had in February sanctioned ten individuals and one organization it said were involved in the crackdown on pro-reform demonstrations that swept through Iran earlier this year. The sanctions targeted parts of the Iranian security apparatus, starting with the Law Enforcement Forces (FARAJA), which were designat

title: Sweden says important that Syria moves in right direction on minority rights
title_en: Sweden says important that Syria moves in right direction on minority rights
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - SwedenвҖҷs foreign minister said on Monday that there is a window of opportunity in Syria regarding minority rights, adding that it is important for the interim government in Damascus to move in the right direction. вҖңWhen I meet with the high-level representatives of the Syrian government, I always raise the issue of the protection of the minorities and it is very important that we now have an inclusive way forward in Syria, and we really have a window of opportunity but it is important that they move in the right direction,вҖқ she told Rudaw. Her remarks came on the sidelines of a European Union meeting on Monday during which the bloc lifted sanctions on Syrian defense minister Murhaf Abu Qasra and interior minister Anas Khattab. The move follows a May 2025 decision by the EU to ease broader economic sanctions, including restrictions on banking, energy, trade, and transport, in support of post-regime stability. The EU has lifted most of the sanctions imposed on Syria during Bashar al-AssadвҖҷs rule. However, it has maintained an arms embargo as well as restrictions on certain technological tools that could potentially be used for internal repression and surveillance. European officials have repeatedly said that lifting sanctions is conditional on verifiable evidence that Syrian authorities are committed to adopting a tolerant and inclusive approach toward the countryвҖҷs ethnic and religious communities and improving the overall human rights situation. Italian Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani told Rudaw on Monday that he had spoken with SyriaвҖҷs interim President Ahmed Sharaa about minorities in the country, noting that Sharaa assured him regarding their rights. вҖңHe told me every Syrian is a citizen of Syria,вҖқ the Italian official said. вҖңI believe in this. This is a good solution,вҖқ he added. Tajani also said that protecting minorities and guaranteeing freedom of religion and the right to study in oneвҖҷs native language are вҖңprinciple[s] good for ever

title: Iran-Iraq committee discusses border security as attacks on Kurdish opposition groups persist
title_en: Iran-Iraq committee discusses border security as attacks on Kurdish opposition groups persist
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - The Iraq-Iran committee formed to implement a 2023 border agreement aimed at disarming and relocating Kurdish opposition groups based in the Kurdistan Region held a meeting in Baghdad on Monday. This comes as Tehran has since the outbreak of the Iran war intensified its attacks on Kurdish dissident groups in the Region, despite their non-involvement in the conflict. The High Committee for the Implementation of the Security Agreement met in the presence of Iraqi National Security Advisor Qasim al-Araji, where delegates from Baghdad and Tehran вҖңemphasized the importance of strengthening joint security coordination, tightening border control measures, and preventing any infiltration or movements by terrorist or armed groups that could threaten security and stability in both countries and the wider region,вҖқ according to ArajiвҖҷs office. The Iranian side reportedly вҖңpresented information related to the activities of certain Iranian armed groups present in the Kurdistan Region,вҖқ the AdvisorвҖҷs office added, noting that вҖңit was agreed to continue coordination and take necessary measures to address this issue in a comprehensive manner, ensuring the prevention of any activities that could threaten regional security or undermine stability.вҖқ For his part, Araji reaffirmed вҖңIraqвҖҷs firm positionвҖқ against вҖңthe use of Iraqi territory as a launching pad for any hostile acts, security operations, or military actions targeting neighboring countries,вҖқ in a way that aligns with вҖңBaghdadвҖҷs efforts to consolidate regional security, respect state sovereignty, and promote shared stability.вҖқ In March 2023, Iran and Iraq signed a security pact under which Baghdad committed to disarming Kurdish opposition groups and tightening security along the shared border. Iran had threatened military action if Baghdad failed to implement the agreement, accusing Iranian Kurdish opposition groups based in the Kurdistan Region of fueling IranвҖҷs nationwide protest movement in 2022 and

title: Kurdish language education is a вҖҳred line for usвҖҷ: Rojava academic
title_en: Kurdish language education is a вҖҳred line for usвҖҷ: Rojava academic
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - A senior official from a university in northeast Syria (Rojava) told Rudaw on Monday that educational institutions in the Kurdish-led enclave will continue providing education in Kurdish, urging the need to safeguard the educational system in place. The remarks come as integration talks between Damascus and Rojava continue, with education as a central issue in the discussions. вҖңWhen it comes to our mother tongue, this issue is related to our national cause,вҖқ said Shervan Mahmood, co-chair of the University of Kobani, stressing that вҖңthe Kurdish language is a red line for usвҖқ and that вҖңwe will never take a step back regarding this language.вҖқ Founded in 2017, the University of Kobani was the first higher education institution in the Kurdish majority city, situated in north-central Rojava. It comprises 20 departments across four faculties and five institutes, offering various undergraduate and postgraduate programs in fields including Kurdish literature as well as arts, humanities, medicine and natural sciences. вҖңSome courses are taught in English as well as Arabic, but the primary language and the adopted language policy at the university is Kurdish,вҖқ Mahmood noted. The University of Kobani launched a PhD program in the Kurdish language last year, according to the universityвҖҷs co-chair, who added that the first masterвҖҷs degree awarded was also in Kurdish language. вҖңFor a language that has reached the level where Master's and PhD programs are opened, I don't believe anyone has the right to put this language under discussion anymore,вҖқ he added. Since 2011, authorities in Rojava have implemented a distinct curriculum featuring instruction in the Kurdish language, separate from the Syrian state system. However, Damascus and the Kurdish-led Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) - which operates as the de facto army in Rojava - reached a landmark agreement on January 29, with significant mediation from the United States through its Special Envoy for Syria

title: Iran hangs top university student on Mossad, CIA spying charges
title_en: Iran hangs top university student on Mossad, CIA spying charges
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - Iranian authorities on Monday executed another prisoner accused of espionage and cooperation with US and Israeli intelligence agencies, according to state-affiliated media, amid a broader crackdown that has seen dozens executed since the start of the war. вҖңErfan Shakurzadeh, son of Jafar, was hanged for the crime of collaboration with the American intelligence service and the Mossad spy service,вҖқ the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC)-affiliated Tasnim News Agency reported on Monday. It added that, according to court documents, he had attempted to provide вҖңclassifiedвҖқ information to вҖңenemy servicesвҖқ while working in a sensitive position at one of the countryвҖҷs scientific organizations involved in satellite technology. Shakourzadeh, a 29-year-old student at Iran University of Science and Technology in Tehran, was detained in early 2025 by IRGC agents on charges of working with hostile states, according to the Oslo-based Hengaw Human Rights Organization. His family was initially given no clear information about his whereabouts or the case against him. Hengaw said Shakourzadeh was held in solitary confinement for nine months and subjected to severe torture by IRGC intelligence agents. Iran has long used the death penalty as a tool of state repression and to stifle dissent. Rights groups say authorities have executed at least two dozen political prisoners since the start of the war on February 28. The US-based Human Rights Activists News Agency (HRANA) reported that Iran carried out 2,063 executions in 2025 - the highest number recorded in around three and a half decades. Human rights organizations said Friday that the Supreme Court had upheld ShakourzadehвҖҷs death sentence and that he had been transferred to solitary confinement in Ghezel Hesar Prison in Karaj, Alborz province, a move often seen as a sign that an execution is imminent. title: Conflict targets Iran, but Iraq bears major consequences
title_en: Conflict targets Iran, but Iraq bears major consequences
summary: For over 70 days, the global community has been held hostage by the escalating conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran. As the world waits to see if a diplomatic breakthrough or a fresh cycle of violence is on the horizon, a startling economic disparity has emerged. Despite being the primary target of international pressure, IranвҖҷs oil industry has proven remarkably resilient, while the production and export capacity of Iraq and the Kurdistan Region have faced a near-total meltdown. During the conflict, exports from Iraq and the Kurdistan Region have plummeted by 86 percent, and production has decreased by 68 percent. In contrast, IranвҖҷs exports have only dropped by 31 percent, and its production by 48 percent. From this perspective, one can argue that the economic damage of the war to IraqвҖҷs revenue sources has been far greater, as if the war were being waged against Iraq itself rather than Iran. Production levels: A comparative look According to Kpler data, IranвҖҷs crude oil production last month was over two million barrels per day (bpd), down from four million in January 2026, before the beginning of the war. However, oil production in Iraq and the Kurdistan Region dropped to 1.35 million bpd in April, down from 4.52 million bpd in January of the same year. Before the war, IranвҖҷs average daily exports stood at 2.2 million barrels. By April 2026, this dropped to 1.52 million bpd (a 31 percent decrease). Meanwhile, Iraq and the Kurdistan Region saw an unexpected and drastic drop of 86 percent. In February, their average exports were 3.56 million bpd, but by April 2026, they hit a record low of only 300,000 to 400,000 barrels. The disruption experienced by the Iraqi and Kurdish oil industries during the 40 days of war and 30 days of ceasefire has been much costlier than for Iran. title: EU to raise Kurdish language rights with Damascus
title_en: EU to raise Kurdish language rights with Damascus
summary: BRUSSELS - European Union foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said Monday that she will discuss Kurdish language rights with Syrian Foreign Minister Asaad al-Shaibani amid tensions in northeast Syria (Rojava) over Kurdish representation and education rights. In response to a question about whether she would discuss Kurdish language rights with Shaibani, Kallas told Rudaw ahead of their meeting in Brussels that "Yes, we will address these issues because also the question of minorities and their rights to have education in their own language in Syria." "Of course, more broader, all the majorities, all the groups that are living in Syria, but of course, the Kurds issue also comes up," she added. The diplomatic push coincides with recent tensions in Rojava over language rights as the interim government in Damascus moves to integrate civilian and military institutions. EU officials have repeatedly said reconstruction aid to Syria is conditional on verifiable progress in human rights, inclusive governance, and the protection of minorities. Recently, Syrian authorities removed a bilingual Kurdish-Arabic billboard from the Justice Palace in Hasaka and replaced it with one written in Arabic and English. The move sparked protests, with residents chanting "Kurdistan" before tearing down the newly installed sign. A similar backlash occurred in Kobane after authorities initially installed locality signs exclusively in Arabic. Kallas also told reporters that вҖңFor us, it is important that the minorities - all the minorities - are taken into account in the government, the inclusivity is important,вҖқ adding that вҖңwe are also offering our help in drafting the Constitution, the different laws that are necessary so that they could actually have elections.вҖқ A Syrian presidential decree issued in January recognizes Kurdish as a вҖңnational languageвҖқ and permits its teaching in schools in Kurdish-populated areas. summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ Щ„Ы• ЪҶЩҲШ§ШұЪҶЫҺЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ЩҮЫ•ЩҲЪөШ§ЩҶЫ•ЫҢ ЩҶШ§ЩҲЫҢ "ШӘЩҲШұЪ©ЫҢШ§ЫҢ ШЁЫҺ ШӘЫҢШұЫҶШұ"ЫҢШ§ЩҶ Щ„ЫҺЩҶШұШ§ЩҲЫ•ШҢ ЩҫШ§ШұШӘЫҢ ШҜШ§ШҜ ЩҲ ЪҜЫ•ШҙЫ•ЩҫЫҺШҜШ§ЩҶ (ШҰШ§Ъ©ЩҫШ§ШұШӘЫҢ) Щ…Ы•ШұШ¬ЫҢ ЪҶЫ•Ъ©ШҜШ§ЩҶШ§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҫЫ•Ъ©Ы•Ъ©Ы• ШҜЫ•Ш®Ш§ШӘЫ• ЩҫЫҺШҙ ЩҮЫ•ЩҶЪҜШ§ЩҲЫ• ЫҢШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ЩҲ ЩҶШ§ЫҢЫ•ЩҲЫҺШӘ "ЩҫЫҺЪҜЫ•ЫҢ ЫҢШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ" ШЁЫ• Ш№Ы•ШЁШҜЩҲЪөЪөШ§ ШҰЫҶШ¬Ы•Щ„Ш§ЩҶ ШЁШҜШ§ШӘШҢ ШЁЫ•ЪөЪ©ЩҲ ШЁЫҢШұ Щ„Ы• ШҰШ§ШіШ§ЩҶЪ©Ш§ШұЫҢ ШЁЫҶ ЩҫЫ•ЫҢЩҲЫ•ЩҶШҜЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ•

summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ ШЁЫ•ШұЩҫШұШіЫҢ ШіЫҢШ§ШіЫ•ШӘЫҢ ШҜЫ•ШұЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҺШӘЫҢЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲШұЩҲЩҲЩҫШ§ ШЁЫ• ШӘЫҶЪ•ЫҢ Щ…ЫҢШҜЫҢШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШұЩҲЩҲШҜШ§ЩҲЫҢ ШұШ§ЪҜЫ•ЫҢШ§ЩҶШҜШҢ Щ„Ы• Ъ©ЫҶШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ШҰЫ•Щ…Ъ•ЫҶЫҢ ЩҲЫ•ШІЫҢШұШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШҜЫ•ШұЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҺШӘЫҢЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲШұЩҲЩҲЩҫШ§ШҜШ§ Щ„Ы• ШЁШұЫҶЪ©ШіЩ„ ЩҫШұШіЫҢ ШіЩҲЩҲШұЫҢШ§ ЩҲ Щ…Ш§ЩҒЫҢ ЩҫЫҺЪ©ЩҮШ§ШӘЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ Щ„Ы•ЩҲ ЩҲЪөШ§ШӘЫ• ШӘШ§ЩҲШӘЩҲЫҺ ШҜЫ•Ъ©Ы•ЩҶ.

title: Trump says IranвҖҷs response to end war вҖҳtotally unacceptableвҖҷ
title_en: Trump says IranвҖҷs response to end war вҖҳtotally unacceptableвҖҷ
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - US President Donald Trump on Sunday rejected IranвҖҷs response to a US proposal aimed at ending months of conflict, calling TehranвҖҷs position вҖңtotally unacceptableвҖқ amid stalled negotiations and renewed warnings from both sides. вҖңI have just read the response from IranвҖҷs so-called вҖҳRepresentatives.вҖҷ I donвҖҷt like it - TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE!вҖқ Trump wrote on his Truth Social platform. TrumpвҖҷs remarks came hours after Iranian state media reported that Tehran had formally delivered its response to the latest American proposal through Pakistani mediators. According to the Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting (IRIB), вҖңThe response of the Islamic Republic of Iran to the latest US proposed text for ending the war was sent today through a Pakistani mediator.вҖқ The state-run outlet added that the proposed framework would focus on вҖңending the war in the regionвҖқ during the current phase of negotiations. According to a report by The Wall Street Journal, IranвҖҷs latest response failed to satisfy a key American demand regarding TehranвҖҷs nuclear activities. The report said, вҖңThe latest response doesnвҖҷt resolve the U.S. demand for commitments in advance on the fate of IranвҖҷs nuclear program and its stockpile of highly enriched uranium.вҖқ The newspaper added that Tehran instead proposed a phased de-escalation plan tied to maritime access and sanctions relief. вҖңInstead, Iran is proposing an end to the fighting and a gradual opening of the Strait of Hormuz to commercial traffic as the U.S. lifts its blockade on Iranian ships and ports,вҖқ the report added. The diplomatic push follows six weeks of intense hostilities between the United States, Israel, and Iran. Washington and Tel Aviv launched a large-scale aerial campaign against Iran on February 28, targeting more than 17,000 sites across the country before a Pakistan-mediated ceasefire took effect on April 8. title: Ship carrying Iraqi oil passes Hormuz in coordination with Iran
title_en: Ship carrying Iraqi oil passes Hormuz in coordination with Iran
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - Iranian state-affiliated media announced Monday that an oil tanker carrying Iraqi crude oil passed through the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday, amid ongoing Iranian maritime restrictions in the strategic waterway. "A VLCC oil tanker named 'AGIOS FANOURIOS I' passed through the route designated by Iran in the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday,вҖқ the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC)-affiliated Tasnim News Agency reported on Monday. It added that the tanker, carrying вҖңIraqi crude oil,вҖқ is currently in the Sea of Oman and heading toward the Vietnamese port of Nghi Son. The passage comes after months of maritime navigation restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz following an Iranian-imposed blockade after the US and Israel launched a wide-scale aerial campaign against Iran on February 28. The conflict lasted more than six weeks before the sides agreed to a Pakistan-mediated ceasefire on April 8 to pave the way for negotiations. Alongside diplomatic efforts, Iran and the US have engaged in reciprocal maritime measures. Tehran has tightened controls on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, while Washington initiated a maritime blockade on Iranian ports in mid-April. The Maltese-flagged tanker departed IraqвҖҷs southern Basra port in late April and is expected to arrive at Nghi Son on May 26, according to data from Kpler MarineTraffic, a vessel tracking service. Iran imposed fees in late March estimated at around $2 million for large tankers carrying approximately 2 million barrels of oil, often described as a levy of roughly $1 per barrel. In early April, Ebrahim Zolfaghari, spokesperson for the IRGCвҖҷs Khatam al-Anbiya Headquarters, said that "brotherly Iraq" would be exempt from the blockade, which he said would apply only to "hostile nations." Despite that, Iraq has struggled to restore oil exports to prewar levels because it relies heavily on foreign vessels for crude shipments. summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЩҫШ§ЩҶЫҢШ§ЫҢ ШҰШ§ШіЫҢШ§ШіЫҺЪө ЩҮШ§ЩҲШЁЫ•ШҙЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҢ Щ„Ы•ЪҜЫ•Ъө ЩҫЩ„Ш§ШӘЩҒЫҶШұЩ…ЫҢ 1001 ШұШ§ЪҜЫ•ЫҢШ§ЩҶШҜШҢ Ъ©Ы• ЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ы•Щ…ЫҢЩҶ ЩҫЩ„Ш§ШӘЩҒЫҶШұЩ…ЫҢ ЫҢШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮЫҢЫҢЫ• ШЁЫҶ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҫЫ•Ш®ШҙЫҢ ШҜЫҢШ¬ЫҢШӘШ§ЪөЫҢ (OTT). ШЁЫ•ЪҜЩҲЫҺШұЫ•ЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ЩҮШ§ЩҲШЁЫ•ШҙЫҢЫҢЫ•ШҢ ШЁЫ•ШҙШҜШ§ШұШЁЩҲЩҲШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШҰШ§ШіЫҢШ§ШіЫҺЪө Щ„Ы• ШұЫҺЪҜЫ•ЫҢ ЩҫШ§Ъ©ЫҺШ¬Ы• Щ„ЫҺЪ©ШҜШұШ§ЩҲЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЩҫШ§ЩҶЫҢШ§Ъ©Ы•ЩҲЫ•ШҢ ШҜЫ•ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶ ШЁЫ•ШЁЫҺ ШЁЫ•ШұШ§Щ…ШЁЫ•Шұ ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫ• ЩҶЫҺЩҲЫ•Ъ•ЫҶЪ©ЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ЩҫШұЫҺЩ…ЫҢЫҶЩ… ЩҲ Ш¬ЫҶШұШ§ЩҲШ¬ЫҶШұЫҢ ШіЫҢЩҶЫ•Щ…Ш§ЫҢЫҢ ЩҲ ЩҲЫ•ШұШІШҙЫҢ ШЁЪҜШ§ШӘ.

title: ЪҶЩҲШ§ШұЪҶЫҺЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЩҮШ§ЩҲШҰШ§ЩҮЫ•ЩҶЪҜЫҢ Ъ©ЫҶШҜЫ•ШЁЫҺШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ•
title_en: Four-way cooperation
summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ ЩҲЫ•ШІШ§ШұЫ•ШӘЫҢ ЩҶЫ•ЩҲШӘ ЩҲ Ш¬ЫҺЪҜШұЫҢ ШіЫ•ШұЫҶЪ©ЩҲЫ•ШІЫҢШұШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• ЩҫШҙЪ©ЫҢ ШҙЫҢШ№Ы•ШҢ ШіЫ•ШұЫ•Ъ©ЫҢШӘШұЫҢЩҶ ЩҮЫҶЪ©Ш§ШұЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ШҜЩҲШ§Ъ©Ы•ЩҲШӘЩҶЫҢ ЩҫЫҺЪ©ЩҮЫҺЩҶШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШӯЪ©ЩҲЩ…Ы•ШӘЫҢ ЩҶЩҲЫҺЩҶ.

ШЁЫ•ЪҜЩҲЫҺШұЫ•ЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ЩҮШ§ЩҲШЁЫ•ШҙЫҢЫҢЫ•ШҢ ШЁЫ•ШҙШҜШ§ШұШЁЩҲЩҲШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШҰШ§ШіЫҢШ§ШіЫҺЪө Щ„Ы• ШұЫҺЪҜЫ•ЫҢ ЩҫШ§Ъ©ЫҺШ¬Ы• Щ„ЫҺЪ©ШҜШұШ§ЩҲЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЩҫШ§ЩҶЫҢШ§Ъ©Ы•ЩҲЫ•ШҢ ШҜЫ•ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶ ШЁЫ•ШЁЫҺ ШЁЫ•ШұШ§Щ…ШЁЫ•Шұ ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫ• ЩҶЫҺЩҲЫ•Ъ•ЫҶЪ©ЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ЩҫШұЫҺЩ…ЫҢЫҶЩ… ЩҲ Ш¬ЫҶШұШ§ЩҲШ¬ЫҶШұЫҢ ШіЫҢЩҶЫ•Щ…Ш§ЫҢЫҢ ЩҲ ЩҲЫ•ШұШІШҙЫҢ ШЁЪҜШ§ШӘ.

title: Iraq revenues fall to half of monthly spending: PM advisor
title_en: Iraq revenues fall to half of monthly spending: PM advisor
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - IraqвҖҷs monthly revenues have dropped to nearly half of its expenditures due to declining oil exports following the US-Iran war, an advisor to outgoing Prime Minister Mohammed ShiaвҖҷ al-Sudani said on Monday, warning that Baghdad may need to resort to domestic or foreign borrowing to sustain public spending. Monthly revenue has dropped to nearly four trillion dinars (over $3 billion), compared to the countryвҖҷs financial obligations, which exceed eight trillion dinars per month (over $6.1 billion), Mudher Mohammed Salih, the premierвҖҷs financial adviser, told Rudaw, adding IraqвҖҷs public finances are facing a вҖңsensitive phaseвҖқ as oil revenues continue to decline. He noted that the spending mainly covers salaries and essential operational expenditures. The decline in Iraqi oil revenues follows disruptions linked to the US-Israel war with Iran, which led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz on February 28 - a key route for Iraqi crude exports. Although a ceasefire is currently in place, issues surrounding the movement of oil tankers through the strategic waterway persist. Salih stressed that the situation currently represents "a temporary liquidity crisis rather than a deficit that threatens the stateвҖҷs continued functionality,вҖқ but warned that prolonged pressure could affect IraqвҖҷs economic stability. For Iraq, before the six-week war, production stood at around 4.5 million barrels per day, with roughly 3.5 million barrels exported daily. Nearly 90 percent of those exports passed through the Strait of Hormuz. However, exports fell to 18.6 million barrels in March, generating about $1.96 billion in revenue, compared with more than 99 million barrels and $6.81 billion in February, according to official figures from the oil ministry. Oil income, which finances more than 84 percent of IraqвҖҷs expenditures, has fallen to around 2.5 trillion dinars, Salih added, noting that the amount is enough to cover only 34 percent of the funds required for public

title: Iraq denies new unauthorized landings after alleged Israeli base report
title_en: Iraq denies new unauthorized landings after alleged Israeli base report
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - IraqвҖҷs Security Media Cell on Sunday denied reports that unauthorized forces had recently landed in the country, saying an incident recently referenced in US media reports took place in March and that Iraqi authorities had already responded to it. The statement came after a Saturday report by The Wall Street Journal alleged that Israel established a clandestine military outpost in IraqвҖҷs western desert to support air operations against Iran. вҖңNo new airborne landings have occurred in the Karbala desert. The incident mentioned in the American media dates back to March 5,вҖқ Saad Maan, head of Iraq's Security Media Cell, told Rudaw on Sunday. вҖңAt that time, the necessary measures were taken regarding that unauthorized airborne landing which took place in the al-Nukhaib desert,вҖқ he added. Nukhayb is a desert region in southwestern Iraq, located in Anbar province near the borders of Karbala and Najaf provinces, along key routes linking central Iraq to Saudi Arabia. According to Maan, Iraqi security and military forces confronted an unidentified force operating without authorization in the area. вҖңIraqi security and military forces were present at the site and clashed with an unknown force whose presence was unauthorized,вҖқ he said, noting that one Iraqi security member was killed and two others wounded in the incident. Baghdad had confirmed the clash and casualties at the time. Maan added that Iraqi authorities have not recorded similar incidents since then. вҖңThrough our follow-ups, it has become clear that during the months of April and May, neither the previous force nor any other unauthorized force has landed, and no similar incidents have occurred. Security forces continue to patrol that area,вҖқ he stated. The Wall Street Journal reported on Saturday that вҖңIsrael set up a clandestine military outpost in the Iraqi desert to support its air campaign against Iran and launched airstrikes against Iraqi troops who almost discovered it early in the war

title: Anfal: the long shadow of a genocide
title_en: Anfal: the long shadow of a genocide
summary: Thirty-eight years have passed, yet a certain smell still hangs over Kurdistan. Survivors remember it clearly: the smell of mustard gas, burned earth, and silence. The Anfal genocide of 1988 was not only a military crime committed by the Baath regime under Saddam Hussein. It was a systematic attempt to erase an entire people from history: their bodies, their language, their villages, and their memory. Between February and September 1988, thousands of Kurdish families disappeared into the deserts of Iraq. More than 4,000 villages were destroyed. Hundreds of thousands were displaced. Up to 182,000 Kurds were killed or disappeared. Men were separated from their families and executed. Women and children were taken to camps. Entire regions of Iraqi Kurdistan were emptied and destroyed. Halabja became the symbol of this horror when chemical gas fell from the sky on March 16, 1988. Within hours, around 5,000 civilians were dead. The images of lifeless bodies on the streets shocked the world for a brief moment. But Anfal was far greater than Halabja alone. The genocide reached nearly every part of southern Kurdistan: Garmiyan, Badinan, Qaradagh, and hundreds of villages whose names survive today only in the memories of the living. The tragedy of Anfal was also a tragedy of international silence. This genocide did not begin suddenly. It was the result of years of dehumanization. For decades, Kurds in Iraq were denied political rights, displaced from their lands, and stripped of their cultural existence. Genocide does not begin with mass graves. It begins with the idea that some people are worth less than others. It begins when a language is forbidden, when identity is denied, and when an entire population is described as a threat. The violence did not end in 1988. It only changed its form. Even today, survivors carry the genocide inside their bodies and minds. summary: ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ ШҜЫҶЩҶШ§ЪөШҜ ШӘШұЫ•Щ…ЩҫШҢ ШіЫ•ШұЫҶЪ©ЫҢ ШҰЫ•Щ…ШұЫҢЪ©Ш§ ШұШ§ЫҢЪҜЫ•ЫҢШ§ЩҶШҜШҢ ЩҲЫ•ЪөШ§Щ…ЫҢ ШӘШ§ШұШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫҶ ЩҫЫҺШҙЩҶЫҢШ§ШІЫ•Ъ©Ы•ЫҢ ЩҲШ§ШҙЩҶШӘЩҶ ШЁЫ• Щ…Ы•ШЁЫ•ШіШӘЫҢ Ъ©ЫҶШӘШ§ЫҢЫҢЩҮЫҺЩҶШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫ• Ш¬Ы•ЩҶЪҜ ШЁЫ• ШҜЪөЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ЩҶЫҢЫҢЫ• ЩҲ ШЁЫ• "ШӘЫ•ЩҲШ§ЩҲЫҢЫҢ ЩӮШЁЩҲЩҲЪөЩҶЫ•Ъ©ШұШ§ЩҲ" ЩҲЫ•ШіЩҒЫҢ Ъ©ШұШҜ.

title: Iran sends response to US proposal via Pakistan
title_en: Iran sends response to US proposal via Pakistan
summary: ERBIL, Kurdistan Region - Iran on Sunday sent its response to the US proposal to end the war to Pakistan, state media reported, days after Tehran said the document was вҖңunder review.вҖқ вҖңThe response of the Islamic Republic of Iran to the latest US proposed text for ending the war was sent today through a Pakistani mediator,вҖқ the Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcaster (IRIB) reported. вҖңAccording to the proposed plan, this phase of negotiations will focus on ending the war in the region,вҖқ the state-run outlet added. TehranвҖҷs foreign ministry spokesperson has stated on Wednesday that вҖңthe US plan and proposal [to end the escalation between the two sides] are still under review by Iran,вҖқ according to the state-affiliated Iranian Students' News Agency (ISNA). Esmaeil Baghaei then informed that Tehran will convey its position to the Pakistani side once the assessment process is complete. The US and Israel launched a wide scale aerial campaign against Iran on February 28, striking more than 17,000 sites across the country over six weeks of hostilities before the sides agreed to a Pakistan-mediated ceasefire on April 8. The truce halted fighting for two weeks and paved the way for negotiations. While the first round of talks concluded without a final agreement on April 11, a second round has yet to take place, with a comprehensive resolution to the conflict still pending. In parallel with the diplomatic efforts, Iran and the US have engaged in tit-for-tat maritime measures. Tehran has tightened its grip on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, while Washington initiated a maritime blockade on Iranian ports on April 13. Earlier in the week, the American website Axios reported that Washington and Tehran are moving closer to agreeing on a one-page memorandum of understanding that could end hostilities and establish a framework for broader nuclear talks, according to US officials and sources familiar with the matter. summary: ШұЩҲЩҲШҜШ§ЩҲ ШҜЫҢШ¬ЫҢШӘШ§Ъө ЫҢШ§ЩҶЫ•ЫҢ ШҰШ§ШұШіЫҢЩҶШ§Ъө ЩҲ ЪҶЩҲШ§Шұ ЫҢШ§ЩҶЫ•ЫҢ ШҜЫҢЪ©Ы•ЫҢ ЪҜЫ•ЩҲШұЫ•ЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲШұЩҲЩҲЩҫШ§ ШҰШ§ШұЫ•ШІЩҲЩҲЫҢ Ш®ЫҶЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫҶ ЪҜЩҲШ§ШіШӘЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЩҒЫҺШҜШұЫҢЪ©ЫҶ ЪӨШ§ЪөЪӨЫҺШұШҜЫҢШҢ ЩҶШ§ЩҲЫ•Ъ•Ш§ШіШӘЪ©Ш§ШұЫҢ ШҰЫҶШұЫҶЪҜЩҲШ§ЫҢЫҢ ЫҢШ§ЩҶЫ•ЫҢ ШұЫҢШ§Щ„ Щ…Ы•ШҜШұЫҢШҜ ЩҫЫҺШҙШ§ЩҶШҜШ§ЩҲЫ•ШҢ ШҜЩҲШ§ЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ШҜЫ•ЩҶЪҜЫҶЫҢ Ш¬ЫҢШ§ШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢШІШ§ЩҶЫ• Щ„Ы• ЫҢШ§ЩҶЫ• Щ…Ы•ШҜШұЫҢШҜЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ы• ШЁЫ•ЩҮЫҶЫҢ Ъ©ЫҺШҙЫ•ЫҢЫ•Ъ© Щ„Ы•ЩҶЫҺЩҲ ЫҢШ§ЩҶЫ•Ъ©Ы• ШЁЪөШ§ЩҲШЁЩҲЩҲЫ•ЩҲЫ•. Щ…ЫҢШҜЫҢШ§Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШҰШ§Щ…Ш§ЪҳЫ• ШЁЫ•ЩҲЫ• ШҜЫ•Ъ©Ы•ЩҶ ЪӨШ§ЪөЪӨЫҺШұШҜЫҢ ШЁЫ•ЩҮЫҶЫҢ Ъ©ЫҺШҙЫ•ЫҢЫ•Ъ© Щ„Ы•ЪҜЫ•Ъө ШҰЫҶШұЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҶ ЪҶЩҲШ§Щ…ЫҢЩҶЫҢ ЩҮШ§ЩҲШӘЫҢЩҫЫҢ Щ„Ы• Щ…Ы•ШҙЩӮЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ШұЫҶЪҳЫҢ ЩҫЫҺЩҶШ¬ШҙЫ•Щ…Щ…Ы•ШҜШ§ШҢ ШұЩҲЩҲШЁЫ•Ъ•ЩҲЩҲЫҢ ЩҫЫҺЪ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҲЩҲЫ•. ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢШІШ§ЩҶЫ• ШҰЫҶШұЫҶЪҜЩҲШ§ЫҢЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ы• Щ„Ы•ЩҶЫҺЩҲ ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫҢ Ш¬Щ„ЪҜЫҶЪ•ЫҢЩҶ Ш®Щ„ЫҢШіЪ©Ш§ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ШіЫ•ШұЫҢ ШЁЫ•Шұ Щ…ЫҺШІЫҺЪ© Ъ©Ы•ЩҲШӘЩҲЩҲЫ•ШҢ ШҜЩҲШ§ШӘШұ ШЁЫҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЪҜШұШӘЩҶЫҢ ЪҶШ§ШұЫ•ШіЫ•Шұ ЪҜЫ•ЫҢЫҺЩҶШұШ§ЩҲЫ•ШӘЫ• ЩҶЫ•Ш®ЫҶШҙШ®Ш§ЩҶЫ•. ЩҮЫ•ШұЪҶЫ•ЩҶШҜЫ• ЪӨШ§ЪөЪӨЫҺШұШҜЫҢ ШұЫ•ШӘЫҢЪ©ШұШҜЩҲЩҲЫ•ШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҮЫҢЪҶ Щ„ЫҺШҜШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ ШҙЫ•Ъ•ЫҺЪ© Щ„Ы•ЩҶЫҺЩҲШ§ЩҶЫҢШ§ЩҶШҜШ§ ШұЩҲЩҲЫҢШҜШ§ШЁЫҺШӘШҢ ШЁЫ•ЪөШ§Щ… ЫҢШ§ЩҶЫ•ЫҢ ШұЫҢШ§Щ„ Щ…Ы•ШҜШұЫҢШҜ ШұЫҺЪ©Ш§ШұЫҢ ЫҢШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШЁЫ•ШұШ§Щ…ШЁЫ•Шұ ЩҮЫ•ШұШҜЩҲЩҲ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢШІШ§ЩҶ ЪҜШұШӘЩҲЩҲЫ•ШӘЫ•ШЁЫ•Шұ ЩҲ ЩҮЫ•Шұ ЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ы•ЫҢШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШЁЫ•ШЁЪ•ЫҢ 500 ЩҮЫ•ШІШ§Шұ ЫҢЫҶШұЫҶ ШіШІШ§ ШҜШ§ЩҲЫ•. ЩҫШІЫҢШҙЪ©Ы•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШұШ§ЫҢШ§ЩҶЪҜЫ•ЫҢШ§ЩҶШҜЩҲЩҲЫ• ЪӨШ§ЪөЪӨЫҺШұШҜЫҢ ШӘЩҲЩҲШҙЫҢ ШІЫ•ШЁШұЫҢ Щ…ЫҺШҙЪ© ШЁЩҲЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҫЫҺЩҲЫҢШіШӘЫ• ШЁЫҶ Щ…Ш§ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ 10 ШЁЫҶ 14 ШұЫҶЪҳ ЩҫШҙЩҲЩҲ ШЁШҜШ§ШӘ. ШЁЫ•ЩҲ ЩҫЫҺЫҢЫ•ЫҢ ШҜЩҲШ§ЫҢЫҢЩҶ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢЫҢ ШұЫҢШ§Щ„ Щ…Ы•ШҜШұЫҢШҜ Щ„Ы•Щ… ЩҲЫ•ШұШІЫ•ШҜШ§ Щ„Ы• 24ЩҖЫҢ Щ…Ш§ЩҶЪҜЫҢ ЩҫЫҺЩҶШ¬Ы•ШҢ ЩҫЫҺШҙШЁЫҢЩҶЫҢ ШҜЫ•Ъ©ШұЫҺШӘ ЪӨШ§ЪөЪӨЫҺШұШҜЫҢ ЪҶЫҢШҜЫҢ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢЫҢ ШЁЫҶ ЫҢШ§ЩҶЫ•Ъ©Ы• ЩҶЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ•. ШҰЫ•Щ… ШҜЫҶШ®Ы• ЫҢШ§ЩҶЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ШҰШ§ШұШіЫҢЩҶШ§ЪөШҢ ЪҶЫҺЪөШіЫҢШҢ Щ…Ш§ЩҶЪҶЫҺШіШӘЫ•Шұ ШіЫҢШӘЫҢШҢ ЩҫШ§ШұЫҢШі ШіШ§ЩҶШ¬ЫҺШұЩ…Ш§ЩҶ ЩҲ ШЁШ§ЫҢШұЩҶ Щ…ЫҢЩҲЩҶШҙЩҶЫҢ ЩҮЫҺЩҶШ§ЩҲЫ•ШӘЫ• ШіЫ•Шұ ЩҮЫҺЪө ШЁЫҶ ШҰЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ Щ„Ы• Щ…ЫҺШұЪ©Ш§ШӘЫҶЫҢ ЩҮШ§ЩҲЫҢЩҶЫ•ШҜШ§ ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢШІШ§ЩҶЫ• ШӘЫ•Щ…Ы•ЩҶ 27 ШіШ§ЪөШ§ЩҶЫ• ШЁЪҜЩҲШ§ШІЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•. ШҜШ§ЩҮШ§ШӘЩҲЩҲЫҢ ЪӨШ§ЪөЪӨЫҺШұШҜЫҢ ЩҲ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢШІШ§ЩҶШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШҜЫҢЪ©Ы•ЫҢ ШұЫҢШ§Щ„ Щ…Ы•ШҜШұЫҢШҜ ШЁЫ•ШіШӘШұШ§ЩҲЫ•ШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ• ШЁЫ• ЪҜЫ•Ъ•Ш§ЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЪҜЫ•ШұЫҢЫҢ Ш¬ЫҶШІЫҺ Щ…ЫҶШұЫҢЩҶЫҢЫҶ ШЁЫҶ ШұШ§ЩҮЫҺЩҶЫ•ШұШ§ЫҢЫ•ШӘЫҢ ЫҢШ§ЩҶЫ•Ъ©Ы•. Щ…ЫҶШұЫҢЩҶЫҢЫҶ ШҰЫҺШіШӘШ§ ШұШ§ЩҮЫҺЩҶЫ•ШұЫҢ ШЁЫҺЩҶЩҒЫҢЪ©Ш§ЫҢЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЫ•ШұЪҶЫ•ЩҶШҜЫ• ЪҜЩҒШӘЩҲЪҜЫҶЪ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ Щ„Ы•ЪҜЫ•Ъө ЩҒЩ„ЫҶШұЫҺЩҶШӘЫҢЩҶЫҶ ЩҫЫҺШұЫҺШІ ШұЫ•ШӘЩҶШ§Ъ©Ш§ШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ•ШҢ ШЁЫ•ЪөШ§Щ… ШұШ§ЫҢЪҜЫ•ЫҢШ§ЩҶШҜЩҲЩҲЫ• "ШӘШ§ЩҲЫ•Ъ©ЩҲ ШҜЩҲШ§ЫҢЫҢЩҶ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢЫҢ Ш®ЩҲЩ„Ы•Ъ©Ы• ЩӮШіЫ• Щ„Ы•ЪҜЫ•Ъө ЩҮЫҢЪҶ ШЁЫ•ШұЩҫШұШіЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШұЫҢШ§Щ„ Щ…Ы•ШҜШұЫҢШҜ ЩҶШ§Ъ©Ы•Щ…". ЩҒЫҺШҜШұЫҢЪ©ЫҶ ЪӨШ§ЪөЪӨЫҺШұШҜЫҢ ЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҺЪ©Ы• Щ„Ы• ШҰЫ•ШіШӘЫҺШұЫ• ШҜШұЫ•ЩҲШҙШ§ЩҲЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҮЫҺЪөЫҢ ЩҶШ§ЩҲЫ•Ъ•Ш§ШіШӘЫҢ ШұЫҢШ§Щ„ Щ…Ы•ШҜШұЫҢШҜ ЩҲ Щ„Ы• ШіШ§ЪөШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШұШ§ШЁШұШҜЩҲЩҲШҜШ§ ШұЫҶЪөЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ЪҜШұЩҶЪҜЫҢ Щ„Ы• ШЁШұШҜЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЩҶШ§ШІЩҶШ§ЩҲЫ• ЩҶШ§ЩҲШ®ЫҶЫҢЫҢ ЩҲ ШҰЫ•ЩҲШұЩҲЩҲЩҫЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶШҜШ§ ЩҮЫ•ШЁЩҲЩҲЫ•. ЪӨШ§ЪөЪӨЫҺШұШҜЫҢ Ъ©Ы• Щ„Ы•ШіШ§ЪөЫҢ 2016ЩҖЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҫЫ•ЫҢЩҲЫ•ЩҶШҜЫҢ ШЁЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶЫ• Щ…Ы•ШҜШұЫҢШҜЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ы•ЩҲЫ• Ъ©ШұШҜЩҲЩҲЫ•ШҢ ЪҜШұЫҺШЁЫ•ШіШӘЫ•Ъ©Ы•ЫҢ ШӘШ§ЩҲЫ•Ъ©ЩҲ ШіШ§ЪөЫҢ 2029 ШЁЫ•ШұШҜЫ•ЩҲШ§Щ…Ы•ШҢ ШЁЫ•ЪөШ§Щ… ШұЩҲЩҲШҜШ§ЩҲЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ЪҶЫ•ЩҶШҜ ШұЫҶЪҳЫҢ ШұШ§ШЁШұШҜЩҲЩҲ ШҰЫ•ЪҜЫ•ШұЫҢ ШұЫҶЫҢШҙШӘЩҶЫҢ ШҰЫ•ЩҲ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢШІШ§ЩҶЫ•ЫҢШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШіШ§ЩҶШӘЫҢШ§ЪҜЫҶ ШЁЫҺШұЩҶШ§ШЁЫҢЫҶ ШІЫҢШ§ШӘШұ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҲЩҲЫ•. ЩҲЫ•Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶЩ…ЫҺЩҶШӘЫҺЩғ ШЁЩҶЩҲЩҲШіЫ• ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШұЫ• ЪҳЩҲЩҲШұЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҲЩҲ Ш®ШІЩ…Ы•ШӘЪҜЩҲШІШ§ШұЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩғШ§ШұШЁЫҺЩҶЩҮвҖҢ ШЁЫ•ШҙЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШІЫҶШұЫҢ Ъ©ЫҺШҙЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ЪҶШ§ШұЫ•ШіЫ•Шұ ШҜЫ•Ъ©Ш§ШӘ

ШҜЩҲЩҲШЁШ§ШұЫ• ЩҮЫ•ЩҲЪөШЁШҜЫ• ШұЫҶЪҳЩҮЫ•ЪөШ§ШӘЫҢ ЩҶЫҺЩҲЫ•Ъ•Ш§ШіШӘ ШЁЩҶЫҢШ§Щ…ЫҢЩҶ ЩҶЫ•ШӘЫ•ЩҶЫҢШ§ЩҮЩҲЩҲШҢ ШіЫ•ШұЫҶЪ©ЩҲЫ•ШІЫҢШұШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШҰЫҢШіШұШ§ШҰЫҢЩ„ ШІЫҶШұШӘШұ Щ„Ы• ШұЫҶЪҳЩҮЫ•ЪөШ§ШӘЫҢ ЩҶЫҺЩҲЫ•Ъ•Ш§ШіШӘ ШҰЫҢШіШұШ§ШҰЫҢЩ„: ЪҜЩҲЩ…Ы•ШІЫҢ ШҰШ§ШіЩҶЫҢЩҶ 99%ЩҖЫҢ Щ…ЩҲЩҲШҙЫ•Ъ©Ы•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ШӯЫ•Щ…Ш§Ші ЩҲ ШӯЫҢШІШЁЩҲЪөЪөШ§ЫҢ ШӘЫҺЪ©ШҙЪ©Ш§ЩҶШҜЩҲЩҲЫ• Щ…ЫҢШҜЫҢШ§ЫҢ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ: Ъ©Ш§ШұШҜШ§ЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ШӘШұЫ•Щ…Щҫ Щ„Ы• ШЁЫ•ШұШ§Щ…ШЁЫ•Шұ ЩҲЫ•ЪөШ§Щ…ЫҢ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ ЪҜШұЩҶЪҜ ЩҶЫҢЫҢЫ• ШіЩҲЩҫШ§ЫҢ Щ„ЩҲШЁЩҶШ§ЩҶ Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҢ ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЪҜЫҢШұЪ©ШұШҜ Ъ©Ы• Ш®ЫҶЫҢ ЩҲЫ•Ъ© ШЁЫ•ШұЩҫШұШіЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШҰЫ•Щ…ЩҶЫҢЫҢ ШЁШ§ЪөЫҢЫҶШІШ®Ш§ЩҶЫ• ЩҶШ§

ШҜЩҲЩҲШЁШ§ШұЫ• ЩҮЫ•ЩҲЪөШЁШҜЫ• ШұЫҶЪҳЩҮЫ•ЪөШ§ШӘЫҢ ЩҶЫҺЩҲЫ•Ъ•Ш§ШіШӘ ЩҮЫҺШұШҙЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШіЩҲЩҫШ§ЫҢ ШҰЫҢШіШұШ§ШҰЫҢЩ„ ШЁЫҶШіЫ•Шұ ШЁШ§ШҙЩҲЩҲШұЫҢ Щ„ЩҲШЁЩҶШ§ЩҶ. ЩҲЫҺЩҶЫ•: AFP ШІЫҶШұШӘШұ Щ„Ы• ШұЫҶЪҳЩҮЫ•ЪөШ§ШӘЫҢ ЩҶЫҺЩҲЫ•Ъ•Ш§ШіШӘ ШҰЫҢШіШұШ§ШҰЫҢЩ„: ЪҜЩҲЩ…Ы•ШІЫҢ ШҰШ§ШіЩҶЫҢЩҶ 99%ЩҖЫҢ Щ…ЩҲЩҲШҙЫ•Ъ©Ы•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ШӯЫ•Щ…Ш§Ші ЩҲ ШӯЫҢШІШЁЩҲЪөЪөШ§ЫҢ ШӘЫҺЪ©ШҙЪ©Ш§ЩҶШҜЩҲЩҲЫ• Щ…ЫҢШҜЫҢШ§ЫҢ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ: Ъ©Ш§ШұШҜШ§ЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ШӘШұЫ•Щ…Щҫ Щ„Ы• ШЁЫ•ШұШ§Щ…ШЁЫ•Шұ ЩҲЫ•ЪөШ§Щ…ЫҢ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ ЪҜШұЩҶЪҜ ЩҶЫҢЫҢЫ• ШіЩҲЩҫШ§ЫҢ Щ„ЩҲШЁЩҶШ§ЩҶ Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҢ ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЪҜЫҢШұЪ©ШұШҜ Ъ©Ы• Ш®ЫҶЫҢ ЩҲЫ•Ъ© ШЁЫ•ШұЩҫШұШіЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШҰЫ•Щ…ЩҶЫҢЫҢ ШЁШ§Ъө

title_en: Report: Israeli Military Base Established on Iranian Border
summary: ШӘЫ•ЩҶЫҢШ§ ШҰЫ•ШӯЩ…Ы•ШҜ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШҙЫҢ ШЁЩғЩҮвҖҢ Щ„Ы• Share on  Facebook Share on Twitter Share on Whatsapp Share on Telegram Share on Viber Share on Messenger ШІЫҢШ§ШҜЫҢ ШЁЪ©Ы• ШЁЫҶ Щ„ЫҢШіШӘЫҢ ШҜЪөШ®ЩҲШ§ШІЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЩ… ЩҮЫ•ЪөЫ•ЫҢЫ•Ъ© ШұЩҲЩҲЫҢШҜШ§! ШҜЩҲЩҲШЁШ§ШұЫ• ЩҮЫ•ЩҲЪөШЁШҜЫ• ЪҳЩ…Ш§ШұЫ•ЫҢЫ•Ъ© ШіЫ•ШұШЁШ§ШІЫҢ ШҰЫҢШіШұШ§ШҰЫҢЩ„ЫҢ. ЩҲЫҺЩҶЫ•Ъ©Ы• ШЁЫҶ ШұЩҲЩҲЩҶЪ©ШұШҜЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ• Щ„Ы•ШіЫ•Шұ ЩҮЫ•ЩҲШ§ЪөЫ•Ъ©Ы• ШҜШ§ЩҶШұШ§ЩҲЫ• ШІЫҶШұШӘШұ Щ„Ы• ШұЫҶЪҳЩҮЫ•ЪөШ§ШӘЫҢ ЩҶЫҺЩҲЫ•Ъ•Ш§ШіШӘ ШҰЫҢШіШұШ§ШҰЫҢЩ„: ЪҜЩҲЩ…Ы•ШІЫҢ ШҰШ§ШіЩҶЫҢЩҶ 99%ЩҖЫҢ Щ…ЩҲЩҲШҙЫ•Ъ©Ы•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ШӯЫ•Щ…Ш§Ші ЩҲ ШӯЫҢШІШЁЩҲЪөЪөШ§ЫҢ ШӘЫҺЪ©ШҙЪ©Ш§ЩҶШҜЩҲЩҲЫ• Щ…ЫҢШҜЫҢШ§ЫҢ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ: Ъ©Ш§ШұШҜШ§ЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ШӘШұЫ•Щ…Щҫ Щ„Ы• ШЁЫ•ШұШ§Щ…ШЁЫ•Шұ ЩҲЫ•ЪөШ§Щ…ЫҢ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ ЪҜШұЩҶЪҜ ЩҶЫҢЫҢЫ• ШіЩҲЩҫШ§ЫҢ Щ„ЩҲШЁЩҶШ§ЩҶ Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҢ ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЪҜЫҢШұЪ©ШұШҜ Ъ©Ы• Ш®ЫҶЫҢ ЩҲЫ•Ъ© ШЁЫ•ШұЩҫШұШіЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШҰЫ•Щ…ЩҶЫҢЫҢ ШЁШ§

title: ШҰЫҶЩҫЫ•ШұШ§ШіЫҢЫҶЩҶЫ• ЩҮШ§ЩҲШЁЫ•ШҙЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ: ЩҮЫҢЪҶ ШЁЩҶЪ©Ы•ЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҢ ШіЫ•ШұШЁШ§ШІЫҢЫҢ Щ…ЫҶЪөЫ•ШӘЩҫЫҺЩҶЫ•ШҜШұШ§ЩҲ Щ„Ы• Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮШҜШ§ ЩҶЫҢЫҢЫ•

ШҜЩҲЩҲШЁШ§ШұЫ• ЩҮЫ•ЩҲЪөШЁШҜЫ• Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮ Щ„ЫҶЪҜЫҶЫҢ ЩҒЫ•ШұЩ…Ш§ЩҶШҜЫ•ЫҢЫҢ ШҰЫҶЩҫЫ•ШұШ§ШіЫҢЫҶЩҶЫ• ЩҮШ§ЩҲШЁЫ•ШҙЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮ ШІЫҶШұШӘШұ Щ„Ы• Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮ Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮ ЩҲ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШЁШ§ШұЫ•ЫҢ ШұЫҺЪ©Ъ©Ы•ЩҲШӘЩҶЫҢ ШҰЫ•Щ…ЩҶЫҢЫҢ ЩҶЫҺЩҲШ§ЩҶЫҢШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ• Щ„Ы• ШҰЫ•ЩҶШЁШ§Шұ 10 ЩӮШ§ЫҢЩ…ЩӮШ§Щ… ЩҲ ШЁЫ•Ъ•ЫҺЩҲЫ•ШЁЫ•ШұЫҢ ЩҶШ§ШӯЫҢЫ• ШҜЫ•ЪҜЫҶЪ•ШҜШұЫҺЩҶ ЩҲЫ•ШІЫҢШұЫҢ ШЁШ§ШІШұЪҜШ§ЩҶЫҢЫҢ Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮ: ШЁЫ•ШҙЫ•Ш®ЫҶШұШ§Ъ©ЫҢ Щ…Ш§ЩҶЪҜШ§ЩҶЫ• ШЁЫҶ ЩҮЫҺШІЫ• ШҰЫ•Щ…ЩҶЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШҜЫ•ЪҜЫ•Ъ•ЫҺЩҶШҜШұЫҺШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЪҶЩҲШ§ШұЪҶЫҺЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЩҮШ§ЩҲШҰШ§ЩҮЫ•ЩҶЪҜЫҢ Ъ©ЫҶШҜЫ•ШЁЫҺШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ• A+ A- ШұЩҲЩҲШҜШ§ЩҲ ШҜЫҢШ¬ЫҢ

summary: ШЁЫ•ШҙШҜШ§Шұ Щ…ЩҲШІЫ•ЩҒЫ•Шұ @bashdarmu ШЁЩҮвҖҢШҙЫҢ ШЁЩғЩҮвҖҢ Щ„Ы• Share on  Facebook Share on Twitter Share on Whatsapp Share on Telegram Share on Viber Share on Messenger ШІЫҢШ§ШҜЫҢ ШЁЪ©Ы• ШЁЫҶ Щ„ЫҢШіШӘЫҢ ШҜЪөШ®ЩҲШ§ШІЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЩ… ЩҮЫ•ЪөЫ•ЫҢЫ•Ъ© ШұЩҲЩҲЫҢШҜШ§! ШҜЩҲЩҲШЁШ§ШұЫ• ЩҮЫ•ЩҲЪөШЁШҜЫ• ШұЫҶЪҳЩҮЫ•ЪөШ§ШӘЫҢ ЩҶЫҺЩҲЫ•Ъ•Ш§ШіШӘ ШіЫҢШіШӘЫ•Щ…ЫҢ ШЁЫ•ШұЪҜШұЫҢЫҢ (ЪҜЩҲЩ…ШЁЫ•ШІЫҢ ШҰШ§ШіЩҶЫҢЩҶ)ЩҖЫ•. ЩҲЫҺЩҶЫ•: ШұЫҶЫҢШӘЫ•ШұШІ ШІЫҶШұШӘШұ Щ„Ы• ШұЫҶЪҳЩҮЫ•ЪөШ§ШӘЫҢ ЩҶЫҺЩҲЫ•Ъ•Ш§ШіШӘ Щ…ЫҢШҜЫҢШ§ЫҢ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ: Ъ©Ш§ШұШҜШ§ЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ШӘШұЫ•Щ…Щҫ Щ„Ы• ШЁЫ•ШұШ§Щ…ШЁЫ•Шұ ЩҲЫ•ЪөШ§Щ…ЫҢ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ ЪҜШұЩҶЪҜ ЩҶЫҢЫҢЫ• ШіЩҲЩҫШ§ЫҢ Щ„ЩҲШЁЩҶШ§ЩҶ Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҢ ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЪҜЫҢШұЪ©ШұШҜ Ъ©Ы• Ш®ЫҶЫҢ ЩҲЫ•Ъ© ШЁЫ•ШұЩҫШұШіЫҺЪ©ЫҢ ШҰЫ•Щ…ЩҶЫҢЫҢ ШЁШ§ЪөЫҢЫҶШІШ®Ш§ЩҶЫ• ЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЩҶШҜШЁЩҲЩҲ ЩҶЫ•ШӘЫ•ЩҶЫҢШ§ЩҮЩҲЩҲ: Ш¬Ы•ЩҶЪҜЫҢ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ ШӘЫ•ЩҲШ§ЩҲ ЩҶЫ•ШЁЩҲЩҲЫ• ЩҲ ШҜЫ•ШЁЫҺШӘ

ЩҲЫҺЩҶЫ•: ШҰЫ•ШұШҙЫҢЪӨ ШІЫҶШұШӘШұ Щ„Ы• Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮ ШҰЫҶЩҫЫ•ШұШ§ШіЫҢЫҶЩҶЫ• ЩҮШ§ЩҲШЁЫ•ШҙЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ: ЩҮЫҢЪҶ ШЁЩҶЪ©Ы•ЫҢЫ•Ъ©ЫҢ ШіЫ•ШұШЁШ§ШІЫҢЫҢ Щ…ЫҶЪөЫ•ШӘЩҫЫҺЩҶЫ•ШҜШұШ§ЩҲ Щ„Ы• Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮШҜШ§ ЩҶЫҢЫҢЫ• Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮ ЩҲ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШЁШ§ШұЫ•ЫҢ ШұЫҺЪ©Ъ©Ы•ЩҲШӘЩҶЫҢ ШҰЫ•Щ…ЩҶЫҢЫҢ ЩҶЫҺЩҲШ§ЩҶЫҢШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЫҶШЁЩҲЩҲЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЩҲЫ•ШІЫҢШұЫҢ ШЁШ§ШІШұЪҜШ§ЩҶЫҢЫҢ Ш№ЫҺШұШ§ЩӮ: ШЁЫ•ШҙЫ•Ш®ЫҶШұШ§Ъ©ЫҢ Щ…Ш§ЩҶЪҜШ§ЩҶЫ• ШЁЫҶ ЩҮЫҺШІЫ• ШҰЫ•Щ…ЩҶЫҢЫҢЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШҜЫ•ЪҜЫ•Ъ•ЫҺЩҶШҜШұЫҺШӘЫ•ЩҲЫ• ЪҶЩҲШ§ШұЪҶЫҺЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЩҮШ§ЩҲШҰШ§ЩҮЫ•ЩҶЪҜЫҢ Ъ©ЫҶШҜЫ•ШЁЫҺШӘ

ШҜЩҲЩҲШЁШ§ШұЫ• ЩҮЫ•ЩҲЪөШЁШҜЫ• ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ Щ„ЫҶЪҜЫҶЫҢ ШіЩҲЩҫШ§ЫҢ ЩҫШ§ШіШҜШ§ШұШ§ЩҶ ШІЫҶШұШӘШұ Щ„Ы• ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ ШҜЫ•ШіЫ•ЪөШ§ШӘЫҢ ШҜШ§ШҜЫҢ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ ШҜЫ•ШіШӘЫҢ ШЁЫ•ШіЫ•Шұ ШіШ§Щ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ЫҢШ§ШұЫҢШІШ§ЩҶЫҺЪ©ШҜШ§ ЪҜШұШӘ ЩӮШ§Щ„ЫҢШЁШ§ЩҒ ШЁЫҶ ШҰЫ•Щ…ШұЫҢЪ©Ш§: ШЁЫҶ ЩҮЫ•Щ…ЩҲЩҲ ШЁЪҳШ§ШұЫ•ЫҢЫ•Ъ© ШҰШ§Щ…Ш§ШҜЫ•ЫҢЩҶ ЩҲ ШіЫ•ШұШӘШ§ЩҶ ШіЩҲЩҲЪ•ШҜЫ•Щ…ЫҺЩҶЫҺШӘ ШӘЫ•ЩҶЪҜЪҳЫ•ЫҢ ШҰШ§ШЁЩҲЩҲШұЫҢ Щ„Ы• ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶШӣ ЩҮЫ•ЪөШ§ЩҲШіШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ ШЁЫҺЪ©Ш§ШұЫҢ ШЁЫ•ШұЫҶЪ©ЫҢ ЩҮЫ•Щ…ЩҲЩҲ Ъ©Ы•ШұШӘЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ЪҜШұШӘЩҲЩҲЫ• ЩҲЫ•ЪөШ§Щ…Ы•Ъ©Ы•ЫҢ ШҰЫҺШұШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫҶ ЩҫЫҺШҙЩҶЫҢШ§ШІЫ•Ъ©Ы•ЫҢ ШҰЫ•Щ…ШұЫҢЪ©Ш§ ЪҶЫҢ ШӘЫҺШҜШ§ЫҢЫ•Шҹ A+ A- ШұЩҲЩҲШҜШ§ЩҲ ШҜЫҢШ¬

title: ЩӮШ§ШҰШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШЁЫҶ ЪҶЩҲШ§ШұЪҶЫҺЩҲЫ•ЫҢ ЩҮШ§ЩҲШҰШ§ЩҮЫ•ЩҶЪҜЫҢ: ЩҶШ§ШЁЫҺШӘ ШіЫ•ШұЪ©ШұШҜЫ•ЫҢ ЪҜШұЩҲЩҲЩҫЫ• ЪҶЫ•Ъ©ШҜШ§ШұЫ•Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ Щ„Ы• ШӯЪ©ЩҲЩ…Ы•ШӘ ШҜЩҲЩҲШұШЁШ®ШұЫҺЩҶЫ•ЩҲЫ•
